Cute, wide-leg, flattering waist, thicker material pants. True to size. My teenage daughter said this style of pants is stylish right now so I'm giving them a try. For reference, I am 4'10" tall and 123lbs and I ordered a size small, which fits pretty accurately (I'll need to hem them, but that's common for me). The style is 'barrel' so they are wide-leg and with the thicker material (almost denim?) they maintain the width. For a petite frame, it kinda makes me look shorter and that's why I can't decide if I like them? That said, the pants are very comfortable. I love the high-waist with button + zipper + cinch tie. The design and pattern is cute. My daughter said she likes them on me (and she's a critic), I haven't decided yet. If you are of average height and like a wide-leg jean, this could work great for you! True to size.

Pretty comfortable and lightweight but wrinkles easily. I wanted a pair of casual pants with pockets to hold my phone. The fit is good but an inch or so too long on me (5’ 4”). There is a drawstring to cinch the waist but I did not know there were drawstrings around the bottom of the pants too. If you cinch the legs, the loop of stretch cord is left hanging out and looks unsightly. My solution was to tuck the loop up under the pants leg and that kept it out of sight. There is one cargo pocket on the right leg and regular pockets at the top. There are no pockets on the back of the pants. I would recommend these pants if you are looking for lightweight, casual pants with storage.

Runs big but so cute and comfy Authentic review incoming! I hope it's . My BFF, age 16, 1999: "What are you going to wear out tonight?" Me, age 16, 1999: "Well, black pants, obviously..." I wear leggings and joggers daily as an ultramarathoner and otherwise very lazy person, but haven't ventured back into flare/wide black pants since literally high school. I gambled on these and they're soooo comfy and cute. They're high-waisted, forgiving, with a wide waistband, nice deep pockets, and I love that they're not an aggressive flare; they're actually more like wide leg pants (or at least they are on me). One thing about the sizing: I'm 5'6" and 135-140 pounds but I'm built like SpongeBob SquarePants so I tend to size up to avoid things being tight in the waist. I can be anywhere from a small to a large depending on the brand. I went with a medium on this one and was hoping it wouldn't be too tight. Umm, quite the opposite. These are big on me, even in the waist (which is no small feat, pun intended), and even though I got the 30" at 5'6", they puddle on the floor a bit as shown in the picture. I don't mind because they are comfy AF and super cute. Except the time the hem got snagged in my rowing machine. Okay, so these will be more like around-the-house pants. Better yet, they'll be my pizza pants (shout-out to Joey Tribbiani). As if I haven't dated myself enough with this review, I can't help but think that these are giving JNCO (IYKYK). I hope I haven't scared off the trendy young'uns from buying these. I promise not to wear them out in public to embarrass you if you promise to stay off my lawn!

Cute and Very Comfortable I am 5'8" and about 130. I am usually a small, but I followed the size chart and ordered a medium in these. When I first put them on, they were perfect and snug, but like many denim items they loosened up and ended the day a little more relaxed looking. The photo is from the end of the day. The color is also a little bit pinker than they look here. I like the look as is, but these are so stretchy I think if I wanted a tighter fit I could have gone with an S.Here is the biggest complement I can give to an internet clothing order. I opened them last night and wanted to wear them immediately. I planned today's outfit around them. I went full hello barbie and paired them with a brighter pink tee. I felt great in them! You do have to watch the fly, as with many zippered high waisted options they need to have the zipper secured properly. And, I kept trying to put my hands into front pockets that aren't there. But I adore these. For this price? Forget about it. Order them.

Nice length crop, light summer weight denim These are a nice light pair of summer weight denim pants. I think they're meant to be crops, but I am 5'3"and these came down to my ankles (which I prefer anyway). The material is generally quite soft but some of the seams are very rough, especially around the waist area. It's like they chose to burn the thread ends rather than tie them off. I would make sure to always tuck in my shirt with these or wear substantial undergarments that come up to the waist (at least) to protect your skin. The thread roughness aside, I like the stretch of these pants. I wish they were a bit more structured in the midsection. Since they are so very high waisted, one would hope they would provide some tummy control, but these just move and stretch with you, which makes them incredibly comfortable. The green is very light and makes for easy pairing with many colors of tops and shoes. I normally wear an 8 or 29 and these pants in a large fit me well.

Def wouldn’t call these baggy, but they’re nice I’ve been wearing these sweats in my rotation at home since August, and they’re not my top pick, but they are pretty nice. To be fair, I like a sweatpant with a really loose fit, especially in the waist. I’m pretty average sized (5’4”, 150lbs, athletic, longer torso and shorter legs), and I would not call these baggy by any means. The waist is definitely tight enough to where you can feel that it’s there; these are more the type of sweatpants that I wouldn’t feel at all uncomfortable wearing out of the house. They’ve got a cinched ankle, a cute tie in the waistband, and good pockets. If you prefer some sweats that hug your bum and won’t slide down at all, or if you are a waif queen, these will definitely be a nice option for you! They are quite soft and comfy in terms of material. As a side note, I know the selling page says to hand wash, but I’ve been tossing these in the laundry with everything else and haven’t had a problem. Like with all my drawstring pants, I tie a little bunny knot with the strings so they don’t get lost in the waistband. The price is right, and for some, these might be the perfect sweaties!

High waisted bell bottoms Black extra small xs fits me 5'2" 104lbs currently, with 25in waist(for high rise pants only). Inseam is roughly 28.5in which is why I must wear 4 inch heels with these pants. I do have long torso and short legs though. They are definitely more bell bottoms than flare pants. I never had a pair of bell bottoms before so when I first tried these pants on I wasn't sold on not being able to see my feet at all. I had to Google and found out it's normal to not see much of your shoes if you're wearing bell bottoms or wide legged pants lol As I tried more tops with these pants, I started to like them. These are truly high-waisted and have a very flattering cut. The fabric is relatively thinner but drapes very well. I don't think it would wrinkle easily. Think spring and summer pants. I tried out different tops with the pants since I never had bell bottoms before, and I found that bodysuits and crop tops probably work the best.Note there are only three belt loops in total.I ended up liking these pants a lot. Sexy and slimming and gives the illusion of longer legs. 4 stars instead of 5 because left pant leg is about 1/5 inch (5mm) longer than right pant leg and ever so slightly bigger leg opening.

High waisted bell bottoms Black extra small xs fits me 5'2" 104lbs currently, with 25in waist(for high rise pants only). Inseam is roughly 28.5in which is why I must wear 4 inch heels with these pants. I do have long torso and short legs though. They are definitely more bell bottoms than flare pants. I never had a pair of bell bottoms before so when I first tried these pants on I wasn't sold on not being able to see my feet at all. I had to Google and found out it's normal to not see much of your shoes if you're wearing bell bottoms or wide legged pants lol As I tried more tops with these pants, I started to like them. Adorable! Great purchase. So I was really confused what size to order because some said order up and others said order down. For reference, I am 5'9 and 125 lbs. I have smaller thighs and often pants can be baggy in that area so I ordered the xs. (grey #1) BIG mistake. Way too small. These have stretch, but not that much. Size chart didn't help. Take me back to Y2K!! I purchased these to wear to a concert: Tori Kelly's "Purple Skies" tour. The album screamed Y2K vibes and... hello, PURPLE!! The fit: The pants fit a little bit snug around my waist. I ordered a Medium, as I'm typically a medium in leggings and sweatpants. Even though they fit snug, I don't know that I would have sized up because everywhere else, the fit was just right.. any baggier I wouldn't have loved the look as much. They come just above my bellybutton, so they'd look nice with a cropped top as well. I'm 5'7" and they hit right above my ankle; however, depending on the shoe you wear, it won't look like you're about to wade through a flash flood. You can also loosen or tighten the draw string at the ankles. The color: These pants are VERY purple. Less of a royal purple color, more like a deep lilac; which I quite like (see the second pic for the true color). So, keep that in mind :) The quality: Without a doubt, these are some pretty well made pants for an amazon find. It's always hit or miss for me when shopping for clothing.. sometimes things are so thin and very see through; but, the fabric used for these pants are on par with any decently priced cargo's from a traditional retailer. I've washed and dried them with no shrinkage or color bleeding and very minimal wrinkles. All of the pockets are functional!

Vibrant, Thin and Long So, I got these for me for an LSU game. I got a size small, and I’m 5’6 and 124 lbs. My mom is also 5’6, and I’m not sure how much she weighs, but she’s not that much bigger than me. I didn’t really like these only because when I went to sit down, the back had no stretch. It wasn’t comfortable to sit in. My mom though, she was able to fill out the back area, and she wears these all the time and doesn’t complain about the back not having that much stretch. It’s a windbreaker kinda material, so there will be no stretch, only in the scrunch waistband. The length was great, had a good baggy style to it and the purple is very vibrant. It’s pretty thin too, but as expected because of the material. The pockets are great. These pants are not see-through.

cute and comfy but the size chart is wrong these pants are cute! the fabric is kind of thin (i ordered black and don't have to wear anything underneath, lighter colours will probably be more see-through) but it does not feel terribly cheap. it is smooth and flows nicely! however, the size chart that is provided is inaccurate. i have a 27 inch waist, and i ordered a small. according to the size chart, i should have ordered a medium. as soon as i opened the package, i knew it was too big. when i tried them on, i could pull the waistband about 2 inches from my body. i can wear a belt to hold them up, but it bunches up slightly around the hips and crotch area in an unflattering way. they are also slightly too long, but that isn't as much of a problem for me as i can wear platforms or pin them. i could probably fit into an xxs better than the size i got. i haven't seen any other reviews mentioning this problem, so i'm unsure if this is just me or not, but keep it in mind if you are planning to buy!!

Fit is great for TALL and feel very comfortable. These hug the hips and waist nicely. I ordered these dressy looking trousers for my granddaughter who I figured would appreciate a nice pair of trousers or pants designed for a taller frame such as she has. It looks like Tall MobPlace did a nice job with these. She notes "The polyester fabric should be durable and easy to care for, unfortunately, the white pants are rather see-through and you can very clearly see where the pockets were put in. The material looks classy and the fit is good. They are very comfortable for fit and easy to get on. They hug the waist and hips and they’re definitely very long and probably will be worn with heels - otherwise they will need to be hemmed up shorter." We considered that since she is an excellent seamstress and could quite easily do that, however the following day she was able to wear them with some 3" heels which she has. I hadn't anticipated that at 5'11" she would have those in her closet but I was obviously out of the loop. It looks like the 34" inseam will be about right with 3” heels. If we had ordered the 32” inseam they would be worn with short heels or flats to fit her correctly. As her grandfather I suggested: "I guess you can just wear the heels and look for tall guys that match up to your higher views and expectations in life."When ordering: Just realize that when they list as a 34" length, that is what they will be. The trousers in all other respects fit and feel real well.

Perfectly Baggy Yet Slimming These cargo jeans give major 90's vibes, and I love pairing them with cropped tops for a throwback look. Honestly, I was skeptical about ordering these since finding the right fit with cargo pants is such a challenge, but these are perfectly slimming yet baggy in all the right places. The high-waisted fit is perfect, and the straight leg that gets baggier towards the bottom is exactly what I look for in cargo pants. I ordered the Medium size with a 30" inseam, and they fit perfectly. For reference, I'm a size 8, 5'6", and I weigh 150 lbs, with a 29" waist and 39" hips. I found the fit to be snug through the waist and hips, which is my personal preference. However, if you prefer a more relaxed fit or find yourself between sizes, you might consider sizing up. I love all the details on these cargo jeans. The elastic waistband is super comfortable and the pockets lie flat and don't add any extra bulk. I chose the black color, and the dark wash is really cool looking. The heavy, thick denim material feels high-quality and durable, like they'll last me for years. I'm already planning on ordering more colors because finding jeans that fit this well can be such a challenge.

Found a new favorite Me: 5’6.5” (5’7” with shoes), 135 lbs, 36-30-37, still haven’t lost the last 15 lbs of baby weight and 10 of those are at my middle; used to be a 0-2 in pants. Pros: good material, stretchy but they have weight to them so they don’t look like stretchy pants. Inseam was actually 33 inches (measuring guide said 32”) which was good for me since wearing flat dress shoes it made them perfect but they are slightly long in just my slippers. They have pockets - real ones! I can actually put my phone in a back pocket. Liberating because I can wear nice pants for work that feel as good as jammies while not feeling bad about my weight. Cons: They are slightly loose on me but not in a falling off way, but just a bit below the waistband they are slightly bunched (due to mom pooch) - however my shirts fully hide this but for others with my body type, I wouldn’t wear my shirts tucked in. The other thing is the cut of the back pockets would look more professional if they were the horizontal slit-type (pocket hidden inside) as opposed to sewn on top jeans-type cut in a pentagon. Again, I wear longer loose tops with cardigans so it is not too noticeable. I just bought another pair in small long because I am for real going to get some more weight off and I fear that if I got down 5 lbs these will be baggy.

Pretty Color; Not Amazing I had higher hopes for this one. I’m 192 pounds and 5’6. I got a size large. What I like about it: the color is beautiful. The fabric is almost silky and super soft. It has pockets. What I don’t love about it: the fit was a bit weird. Because of the waist band, I expected it to hold in your tummy a bit but it doesn’t. The pants are not see through but the fabric was thin enough that if I pulled up the waist band to be flattering on my waist, you could see my cellulite on my thighs. If you’re smaller and don’t have cellulite, these would probably work great for you! The problem is if I would have sized down one, the waist band probably would have been more flattering but then the pants would have been too tight for my liking on my thighs. All together: for me personally: they’re okay, but not super flattering. I probably won’t be wearing them out of the house, but they’re super comfortable for at home, they’re loose on the legs and it’s a silkyish stretchy material.

Great comfort for curvy figures! I had these on my Amazon Wish List, and my sister bought them for me as a gift. They are even better than I expected! I have a curvy figure with a small waist and larger hips/bottom (plus post-menopause belly), and these fit perfectly. I chose medium, my usual size in pants. The waistband is snug without being tight, and the length grazes the floor with shoes. (For reference, I am just under 5'5", 152 lbs.) These pants are comfortable enough for me to wear when teaching yoga but also dressy enough looking for me to wear to work. I'm thinking about getting more colors! UPDATE, 8/8/24 I've now owned these for about 8 months; I've probably worn them 6-8x. I am still really happy with the comfort and the fit. However, I've noticed that after washing, there is some pilling to the fabric - I can feel it when I run my hands down the front. I had thought about getting these in gray as well, but now I'm hesitant because I'm not sure how they will hold up.

Flattering dress pants These pants are very flattering, with a pleated front, imitation back pockets, and a nice drape. The fabric is a slinky, dressy material that has enough weight to drape smoothly and not bulge stiffly at the hips. I love that they have the impression of back pockets, which creates a nice silhouette; you can see from my images that they're a sewn 'fake' that doesn't actually have any function beyond looks. The waist is elastic in the back, with a zipper and tab closure in front. The size large fits as expected at the waist, and is about 29" inseam. The pink color is a nice cool tone. Overall, I'm really impressed with these pants and so pleased I decided to order them for spring!

Good set for lounging at home I am an apple shaped, mid-sized curvy girl, 5'5" with waist 38, Hips 46, band 35, bust 42, weight about 195lbs, Bra size 38DDD. I am right on the edge between a size large and an XL, short torso, average inseam. The size chart doesn't have a hip measurement which is what I would have used to choose a size, so I went with the large. The fabric is pretty stretchy, so they fit fine but I probably would have preferred an XL to prevent pulling at the pockets. The fabric is a tad thin, so personally wouldn't wear them out of the house, but they are great for pajama/lounge pants. It is nice to have the pockets to slip my phone in. They are pretty long on me, so I think they could work even for some of the taller girls. Overall I think this is a good set for the price.

CONS: They are a bit baggy as stated, so not as flattering of a fit as other more sexy thin style jeans. Good value and could make a nice gift too!

only long on short people these pants are "meh" on our daughter. She's 5'6' and these sit at her natural waist and are barely long enough to meet her shoes. From the first picture on the listing, I thought they would have more shape than they do. I thought there would be more Also, pay careful attention - length is from top to bottom, not the inseam.

Arrived stained, but worked out I got these jeans for my daughter and she really likes them. They were a little disappointing at first because when they arrived, they had a weird brownish-black streak stain on the bottom of the left pant leg. Luckily with stain remover, we were able to get the stain out in the washing machine. She likes the baggy fit of these jeans and says they are true to size. They can be worn low-waisted or mid-rise, whichever works/fits better - and they can be styled with a variety of tops for that urban streetwear look. My daughter is 5’8 and often finds it difficult to get pants that are long enough. She says these are the perfect length and aren’t too short or too long. Happy they worked out with the stain and all - would have otherwise been 5 stars.

Cute high waisted pinstripe pants These pants are so cute on. They have a highwaisted, wide legged design w/ a pleat above each leg. They have a button closure & half of the waistband is elastic to help w the perfect fit. I’m typically an 8/10 or medium in pants, but after checking out the measurements on the size chart I decided to order a size 6 & they fit perfectly. So with that being said, I think these run a little big, but as long as you choose your size based on the seize chart measurements you should be good. The length of these run long, but they’re perfect w heels which is how I prefer to wear them anyway. Not to mention, im on the short side at 5’2, so these are tall girl friendly. The material is on the thinner side,, so unfortunately these may not be the best to wear during the winter, but I plan on getting tons of use out of them before it gets too cold. The material also doesn’t have any stretch, but these are still comfortable due to the baggier fit. I love the classic pin stripe design, but the thin white stripes don’t stand out as much as some other pinstriped designs. Regardless they still have a great look & a white top will easily help to make the pinstripes more noticeable. These seem to be pretty well made too, as I have not come across any flaws. Overall these are great pants that are both cute & comfortable & I definitely recommend them.

Warm and Comfy I ordered these as I needed more warm clothing for the weather change. I’m 5’6 and my measurements are 37-31-44. The large fit. I love how warm they are. The fleece material inside is soft and not itchy. The pockets are perfect and deep enough to hold a cell phone and car keys. The product is advertised as high waisted, but they did not fit high waisted on me. The material itself is thick and of good quality, so they will keep you warm. My only complaint is the price as this is kind of pricey for basic sweatpants. When I say basic, I mean appearance and design. My other issue is that the pants shed a lot on the inside. Hopefully after washing them a few times, the lint will stop coming off on the inside. Overall, I’m happy with the item.

Coooooozy mom sweatpants These Tronjori Womens High Waisted Pull on Baggy Fleece Sweatpants Fall Winter Athletic Jogger sweats are "mom jeans" 80's style fit in the waist for sure! I'm 5'4" and 160 lbs. They're too big for me but that's not a negative. I'd rather have too big than tight and uncomfortable and comfy these ARE. * To start with the beige color is beautiful. It's a soft, creamy looking, light beige. * The outter fabric is very soft. Inner fleece is soft and fuzzy. * These are cozy and warm pants. I can feel a definite difference in how warm I feel with them on. * Side pockets....yea! * Wide elastic waist band. Elastic is soft and zero stiffness to it. There's a tie also in the waist band. * Leg bottoms are elasticized also. * Good stretch in the fabric overall. * Runs big. But I'm a bit petite though I don't generally wear petite sizes. These sweats fit me a bit too large but they fit overall. No saggy bottom. Even though I need to pull them up high on my waist I have no complaint because that's an extra warmth layer on my low back and sides. I generally need a size short in jeans so too much length isn't a shocker to me. Definitely recommend.

Great sweats for cold weather Listen to the measurements and size chart! I typically wear a size 18 in all it's variances from 36-39 inch waist. I ordered these in a 2XL and fit a comfortable looseness around my waist, with enough room to layer under them if I needed to. The legs and pockets are BIG and comfortable. I can store my phone, wallet, whatever else I need in the pockets and probably store even more. If you're looking for very warm sweats, these are it! I experienced no shrinkage in the wash, and I line dried so I can't say anything about dryer effects. Like most sweats these get little lint balls everywhere inside of them, so don't be surprised if you have any leg stubble, you'll have blue fuzz all over them!

Perfect for a stylish shortie! I’m curvy and 5’1”. There’s almost NEVER a pair of pants I can get off the hanger without having to alter them. These pants are THEM. I got the pants for a Dapper Day look, so I was walking around Disneyland all day, but they were super light and comfortable, pretty resistant to wrinkles (and sweat!) through a whole day outside. They are a little wide for a shortie, but I loved them so much I bought the pink ones too!

I plan on getting other colors because these are now ESSENTIAL in my life. Don't walk, don't run, heckin' SPRINT to get these.

OK for petite As an elder millennial my go to pant style has been slim or skinny, so I decided to try the current looser trouser pant trend and bought these in xs short in mocha. I'm glad I did! The quality is pretty good for price (got it on prime day) and the style is nice. My only concern, which is very specific to me - it's VERY hard to shop my size as I'm 4'9" and about 95 lbs. I'm extra petite. Rarely anything fits me perfect. The wide leg and pleats do kind of overwhelm me. But... I also feel I can pull it off as long as the rest of my outfit/top is fitted to counterbalance. Just take a look at the pics and you'll see yourself. I got the short length and while it should hit my ankle its full length on me and even when I'm wearing 2.5 inch heels (again, see pic). So, while my purchase isn't a miss, it's not perfect hit either but that's cause of my size. I'm still going to qear the pants tho! :)

A bit sheer but cute These are true to size and true to color. Comfy. More mid rise than high rise on me (at least an inch below my waist, but I have a long torso) I’m 5’11 and 150lbs and got a medium. I find the red to be a bit sheer so I’d guess lighter colors are even more so. They also get attract a lot of static (or maybe that’s a me thing)Fairly priced

Get SMALL if you're 5'7 and around 119 pounds I read through all the reviews and was stuck between ordering XS and SM. I went with XS bc I read a review that says there is a give bc of the elastic in the back and that was a mistake. It was too small. There is no give at all. The elastic is more for the aesthetic. Technically it could have worked bc it "looked fine" but it felt too tight for me and was squeezing my fat to spill over. If you enjoy that feeling of constriction, then XS can work for you. I ordered SM after and it fits perfectly. I wear them high waisted and it's still pretty long but was able to wear it with tory burch flip flops without it touching the floor. Quality is eh but it IS about $35 bucks for trouser style. Pretty heavy despite the thinness. You have to wear a thong or no show, bc your panty definitely shows through. And the clasp to close your pants is sown in an area where it pulls weird and is slightly unflattering. Only concern now is how they will look after they are washed and dried... Afraid it will be super wrinkly and hard to straighten out. But as for now, I think it's worth it for $35 bucks. Better than spending $250 for decent trousers that you can only dry clean. Hope my review helped!

The “long” is truly long! As a tall girl ™️, I saw the “long” option and had to give these a try & back. I’m 6ft, with a 37” inseam. “Long” is so often a joke. But the large/long are 45” waist to floor, with a 30” inseam plus 13” rise… (obviously with this style the crotch is dropped). I like the weight of the material and they felt comfortable but these wide leg pants are actually so wide, that with me having very wide shoulders & no curves on bottom (I have no waist or hips & a tiny butt lol), they kind of drown me. If you have a different body type I think they’d look really cool. I am going to recommend them to my also-tall but different-shaped sister. And I know I appreciate when other long-legged tall girls speak up about pants, so I wanted to let you know my findings!

Long enough at 5’8” I ordered the beige (will need to wear nude undergarments as my black undies show through in the back) in a size L and they are surprisingly long enough for me at 5’8”! They touch the top of my feet so I’ll be wearing with flats or low heels. I’m 180lbs, 33” waist and usually a size 12. I debated between getting a L or XL and am fine sticking with the L. It has enough room without squeezing & giving a muffin top. Id prefer a button instead of just the clasp closure as it can make the waistband look a little pulled. Doesn’t bother me much though. I’m concerned the pants will be staticky and much too cold in the winter as the fabric is thin and cool. All in all, a good buy.

Nice work/dressy pants! I went by the measurements and the fit is great. Maybe just a titch long (I'm 5'6", waist 35, and got XL) but there is enough room in the rise that I can wear them high at my natural waist and they don't drag on the ground. They have belt loops, nice deep pockets, and a flat hook and zip closure on the fly. There's elastic on the back of the waistband. The material is thin suiting, kind of a drapy polyester twill, it has a flat, cool, smooth surface. It drapes great, doesn't seem to wrinkle, and since it is all-polyester, it does need a good spray of Static Guard to keep it from clinging. They flow when walking and are comfortable to wear, though the surface is kind of cool and slick. The pant legs are the same width all the way down, with two pleats in the front gathering the excess fullness to the waist. The back has no pleats and is thus less voluminous, so it shows the curve of the butt a bit more. I like the fullness of these and the material won't wrinkle easily, so they'd be nice for travel.

Fit great!! These fit SO good. I'm 5'3, 144lbs and got a small short. The length is great to wear with a flat or a heel. These are pretty lightweight so they are great to wear during the warmer months. If you run cold like me, you might be a little chilly wearing these in the winter.

Great pants These pants are just great. The quality, material, and fit are all excellent. I bought two different pairs, the blue and the camel. They are a little too long, even though I bought the "S Short" size. For reference, I am 4'11" and weigh 114 lbs.

Super flattering but size down! I'm 5'10 usually a size 10/30 in pants, the large was pretty big on me. Medium long was perfect, I think I may get these tailored just a smidge so they aren't dragging on the ground. 10/10

Tronjori Palazzo Pants I really read through the reviews - especially the reviews on those who were 5'10 - 5'11". I'm 5'11" and 165 lbs. I usually buy large in pants to accommodate my hips. Some suggested ordering 1 and/or 2 sizes up. I ordered 2 sizes up (olive) and found the pants to be too large. I also ordered a black pair - one size up size and the fit is fine, but I think I could have ordered a large and would have been okay. However sometimes going with the larger size you may gain a tad more in length because the fabric drapes versus fitting too snugly. The hem of the pants come right at my ankle. I would have liked them a tad longer. I may let the hem out or embellish the hem some way. Nevertheless, the black pair looks great with a pair of strappy sandals with a heel and are comfortable and classy looking.(Addition) I did add some beautiful trim (that I purchased from JoAnn Fabrics) to the black pants to lengthen them. Although some are wearing palazzo pants at the ankle, I prefer to wear mine full length. I am totally pleased with the results.

Size comparison For anyone on the fence between 2 sizes, hopefully this comparison helps. I ordered a black S and mocha M short. For reference, I’m 5’4, 126ish lbs. The black S came to my belly button and was a little tight but they have elastic in the back so there is stretch. The bottom was just barely on the ground. The mocha was about an inch below my belly button and an inch above the ground. It’s really a preference but I felt like the mocha made me feel frumpy so I’ve decided to return them and stick with the S.

Great product for the price! Way better than expected. The fabric has a nice weight to it. It is not see through. The color is pretty accurate from the images. I’m 5’2” and I got the short length and it exposes a small portion of my ankles. Definitely not touching the floor which is what I wanted. It flows really nicely. It is high waisted so the zippers are long, but when you sit, it doesnt bulge that much. This sits where my muffin top is and it’s pretty snug but not tight that i cant eat so you may want to size up depending on your size. Pockets are deep enough to fit an iphone 12 pro without it peaking. There are no back pockets unfortunately. Would definitely buy again.

Get them!!! Get them. I wear these all the time with everything! From the office to off duty. They give you an instant fashion boost when you put them on. I also love that they are super comfy and they don’t wrinkle easily. Pockets are always a plus. As far as I know, they are not see-through. I usually check every time I wear something dark underneath. They are a lightweight fabric. There are some defects in the sewing, but they aren’t super noticeable. I had some overlapping stitching at the bottom of the pants near the seams but you wouldn’t notice normally. I do wish the pants fastener had a button in addition to the metal slider one. I’m 5’4” and I usually prefer to wear something with a slight heel with these or they may drag on the floor.

Almost perfect ….. Pants fit well….I ordered them in 3 different colors. I’m 5’6” and weigh 114 lbs and I ordered a size small. I’m typically a sz 4 in pants and with chunky boots I will not have to hem them. I like the elastic back. Front closure is a little “fragile” so use care when opening and closing. My only complaint is that the pockets are lined with a white fabric - not the same fabric as the pants. When you put them on you can see a thin line of white “outlining “ the edge of the pocket. This is true for the 3 colors I ordered (mocha, brown sugar and olive). I can’t speak to the other colors but I would assume it is the same.

I LOVE THESE PANTS They’re lightweight, so perfect for modest outfits in hotter seasons! I love the triangle shaped folds, which hides my mom pooch. It’s also great for us tall girlies (I’m 5’9). For pants, I’m usually a size 10-12 and an XL fits great. The only downsides is that the pant legs are super wide and almost looks blocky. And that it SUPER high waisted so the crotch part hangs pretty low. But all and all, I’m keeping it! Love the look!

These pants are EVERYTHING. Great for petites! I can’t say enough good things about these pants. I am petite (barely 5’1” and 103 pounds) and bought the camel color in XS short. They are so classy and make me feel like Katharine Hepburn. The material is luxurious and they hang perfectly. I love them so much that I bought them in the “XS short” size in ivory and in “XS” regular length in yellow and army green. The XS short is the perfect length for flats and sandals and the regular length is great with heels. They are pretty wide, so if that’s not your thing, you may want to steer clear. Otherwise, say hello to the chicest pants in your closet.

Pear shapes rejoice! I agree with others to focus on your waist measurement - these are true wide leg pants. They're extremely flattering and comfy - my waist is 30" and my hips are 42" but I opted for the L because I typically don't trust anything that says I can wear M bottoms. They're a smidge loose at the waist but the elastic keeps them in place and I like the extra room. They're a little longer than I would have liked - they hit the floor for me and I'm 5'6". Also, the material is polyester and it does show - if they were a cotton blend it would be 5 stars from me.

Very Comfortable! This is my first time giving wide leg pants a try and I’m happy with my purchase. For reference, I’m 5’7, 200lbs with a long torso. Decent Pants 5'6" Waist 31" Hips 39" I got a large. The pants are decent. I am not a huge fan of the material (it is light and breathable but I think it is the type to rub and fray against a purse or between your thighs or something like that, but only time will tell). The cut is fantastic though! The waist line itself has little to no stretch, but the wide leg allows for plenty of thigh room so you don't really need it. I got a large and sizing down or up would not fit for sure. My one complaint is that I feel like the seam on the backside is a tad asymmetrical, like I have to keep twisting the pants so it will be in the right place. They came pretty wrinkled I don't know how the material would take to a steaming yet. Overall, if you're looking for cheap pants that look alright I would recommend these.

I have no luck on Amazon with clothing; Most has a poor fit/cut or is too big for me. These were amazing! -fabric is similar to rayon - which I love for keeping pet hair off me. Absolutely Fantastic Pants Honestly, a 5/5 review for these pants. I bought them looking to supplement my flowy pants wardrobe :) I wasn't compensated in any way for this review. Lets start with how they feel: quality. How they look: professional, fantastic. Can be dressed up or down, a button up looks good tucked in, too. How they fit: exactly as ordered. I got the XS, my waist is 24", the band hugs perfectly. There's a clear button and a clasp on the inside to make sure the pants stay on. Length: adequate. I'm 5'7", and I often wear these pants with a pair of booties to get that shorter look that's "in" now. Just one thing - the rule of everything flowy - to make them look more professional, iron them, the 5 minutes doing so is absolutely worth it. I wore these to several events and many of my lady friends asked where I got them, they ordered some and were happy as well :) 5 stars. Stop shopping around, buy these. Totally worth it.

Great pants. Still returning. I'm writing this review for 2 reasons. 1. There is plenty of stretch in the waistband and there is no need to size up. If you size up there is way too much fabric to your proportions. I know because I ordered a larger size first and it was far too big. I'm 5'0.5" and the small short hit right at the ground. The fabric has a nice light weight for spring/summer but does wrinkle. On to 2. As you can see from the photo there was a child's sock in the pocket as well as a used tissue. The item had obviously been worn and washed. It had no tags and was not in the original packaging. I know what it should look like because as I said earlier, I originally ordered the larger size. I know people return things. I do it frequently, but this was obviously worn AND washed. I could tell from the tissue because it has that thin feeling of going through the wash. I'm sending it back even though it's the right size and I like the item because i refuse to purchase an item for full price that's already been worn and laundered.

Obsessed! (with the light grey) Updated bc I am not exactly thrilled with the buffalo checked ones I bought these because reviews said they were lightweight and I was looking for comfy lounge around pants for summer. I was not disappointed! Love the pockets, love the weight of the fabric, love that they’re soft. They were a bit stiff when I first got them but after one wash they softened up quite a bit. I ordered up a size because of reviews that say they shrank a bit in the wash. I recommend you do the same because they do, indeed, shrink in the wash. I’m 5’4” and about 130. I ordered a large and after washing, they’re perfect. You can’t beat the price so I highly recommend that you get a pair while you can. I originally ordered light grey and came back today to order some additional colors only to notice that many of them are no longer available in sizes I would buy :( I’m hoping they’ll restock because I could absolutely live in these. VERY IMPORTANT UPDATE!!! I received the black and white buffalo checked ones a few days ago and they are a completely different fabric. The grey ones that I fell in love with are 60% polyester/35% cotton/5% spandex. The buffalo check ones are 95% polyester/5% spandex. They are neither light nor are they breathable. I ordered a large again because I thought they’d be the same as the light grey (silly me for thinking that ordering the same pants in the same size but in a different color would result in getting pants made of a completely different fabric.) They obviously did not shrink when washed and I’m swimming in them! If you order that color, you may want to size down BY A LOT.

10/10 for this Gen X lady!! For context I'm 5'7 190# 52 yo... I am very picky about what I wear for many reasons, first being I am a gen xer in my 50s which means I need to still be stylish and somewhat boho but respectable for my age! Iykyk! So I need my pants to be comfy, lightweight, extra cute but not too young and wearable to the office as much as home. I have tried at least a dozen different pairs of pants from Amazon this year and most of them have gone right back to the return pile. So when the shipment of these rust bread linen pants came I rolled my eyes and through the bag on my bathroom dressing table without a second thought. Fast forward to this morning... At the moment I tried them on a ray of light entered the room and there rose a choir of angels singing.... Yes! These are PERFECT in every way! Color is interesting, stylish yet subtle!! Wide leg not shapeless! Perfect hit at ankle! Pockets but not obvious! Elastic waistband provides support, not bulge!!! I love them enough and I have struggled enough to find the right pair that I decided to actually post a photo of me wearing them which made me cringe but I want you to be happy as I am right now! Omw to order one(or two) in every color....

The good, the bad and the rest Probably closer to a 4.5 really. I went with the Fit Type: 28" Inseam, Size: X-Large in Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. The fabric on these pants reminds me of the polyester pants my mother would wear to work in. It's rather a bit of a heavier weight with no stretch other than the back elastic band. The feel is smooth and it's so loose that it doesn't really come in contact with the skin much at all. When it does, like when sitting, it hasn't aggravated my sensitive skin. It has been through the washer and dryer, than sat over night in the dryer before I folded them up and stored in a basket for a few days before I got to this review. Yes, they are a bit wrinkled but not as badly as I would have expected given the way I treated them. They do iron out well. Otherwise, there was no harm in the cleaning cycle, didn't bleed, shrink or twist, pill or pick up excess lint. Really what drew me to these pants was that they were different. Yes, they are a bit large on me, but I like to order a size larger so I can alter if needed. Only minor alterations will be needed to make these a really nice set of slacks for me. That's the best compliment, in my opinion, if it's worth messing with to make them exactly what you want and these feel like they are. Overall, I'm happy with them but can't see wearing them in warmer weather. Peace Peace

Long Pants I ordered size XL in Navy blue. I am 5'10" and the length is decent. The material is very light weight and see-through, was hoping to wear them to work but with them see-through I am not so sure unless I wear a long enough shirt to cover the butt area, so I feel buying pants locally would be better as it's hit-n-miss with ordering clothes online that you can't see in person until they arrive, hence the 2-star rating on these.

LOVE these pants This brand is quickly becoming one of my favorite Amazon clothing brands for office wear. I ordered similar pants to these without pockets a few months back which I LOVE. …..Then I saw the same thing with pockets??? I couldn’t order them fast enough. The large fits me perfectly. With a 29” inseam, they are the perfect length on me for both flats or heels. The comfort of yoga pants that are dressy enough to wear to the office. As for the pockets? These are good sized pockets that can hold my smartphone! Incredible!!!The pleat is permanently stitched, not just pressed. Construction and fabric quality is more than acceptable at this price point. These are the perfect low maintenance office pant.Don’t pass these up. Order them in every color. You won’t regret it!

Classy looking comfortable pants Overall, I like these pants. I am 5’5” and 135 pounds, and medium size fits me perfectly. They are a bit too thick for that. The material is stretchy and is of a good quality. It is thick and heavy, which would probably help the pants retain the shape quite well after repeated machine-washing.

Nice pants but too long… I liked the material and flow of the pants. They are well stitched and are stretchable. The only thing is they are very long for me I’m 5’3 and ordered medium which is the usual size for me but still they are long. I wish they had reasonable length to wear them easily.

Way too short! I’m 5’8’ and ordered an XL. These are seriously like way too short. You can see in the pic. I did have to been over a bit to get the pic but they are still a good 4-5 inches short. Material Content Not As Advertised This is listed as 80% linen, 20% cotton, but the pants that I received were 40% linen, 60% cotton (assuming by "lines" they meant linen). The only reason I bought these pants was because of the high linen content. This explains why some reviewers think they feel more like cotton pants - because they are 60% cotton! They are also very small. Short and much tighter than shown in the photo. I am 5'7" with 41" hips - I ordered the size M, as per their size chart, a M fits up to 42.91" hips. They are very snug around the hips - if I was any bigger, I would be afraid to sit down for fear that they would tear. They are snug enough that the waitband pulls down in the back, and the pockets don't lay completely flat, because the fabric is pulling across my butt. The length reaches to about 1" above my ankle bone if I wear these at my natural waist. I'm afraid to wash them, because if they shrink, I won't be able to wear them. Probably will be returned, even though I like the color, and they seem to be made well.

Run and grab these and in multiple colors I absolutely love how comfy and stretchy these barrel jeans are. I’m short 5’2 and the are the perfect length. I got the xxl, was unsure how stretchy these would be. They run big but that’s how you style them. I’m 210 lbs for reference. The pics really don’t do them any justice. I also bought the washed grey or black.

Adorably comfortable I didn’t have high hopes but I want these in every color!! I’m 5”2” approx 110lb . I have a 27” inseam and these are still a bit cropped although I like to roll a little as you see in the picture . This midrise is perfect on me, as I can not wear high rise! The small is perfect!

The black looks good but it does smell like strong chemicals, I guess that's a thing with black denim. I washed it once already on cold, and still smell it, so I'll wash them again The only other thing I don't really like is that the front pockets keep trying to puff up and out. I might try a safety pin on the inside so they stay down I think $36 is a good price for these, that's the price as I'm writing the review. For size reference I'm in the Large, I'm a size 10. 5'8", 169lb, mid waist is 33.5 and my hips are 40.

Best barrel jeans *update* 10/10, absolutely LOVE these. I’ve tried MANY barrel jeans on Amazon at this point and I think these are my fav so far. Primarily because they have some stretch and are the perfect length! I’m 5’7” for reference and they are slightly cropped without being too cropped and riding too high. They are also mid rise on me, making them flattering and comfortable! I’ve found that many others are so tight and rigid in the waist but too wide everywhere else and these fit like a glove, in the most flattering way…. They fit my waist so comfortably, but clearly look like barrel jeans without being too wide and crazy looking. Also, size down!! I am typically a medium, but ordered a small since someone said they’re stretchy and these fit SO well. The only gripe I had is that they are slightly darker than pictured… but they do have the sort of acid wash/worn look to them, like the photos show, and so I am pleased with that. I’ve already put another color in my cart and will likely buy them asap… the price is also good for the quality and fit. Stoked to wear these! —an additional bonus is that they came quickly, within a couple days. They didn’t have any odd smells to them, so I am super happy! Don’t question it, get them! (They look even darker in my mirror pictures due to poor lighting, so they are a smidge lighter than that) ***update! I bought a second pair in a lighter color and I love them just the same. They’re the best. I wear them ALL the time. They’re my new everyday jean. They’re sooo comfy but so flattering. I did have to have my mom sew and fix the pockets so the lining didn’t keep showing, but it was an easy fix and they never show anymore!

I was curious how this cut of jean would fit me but wanted to give it a try. I actually loved it. The jeans are well made and run pretty close to size. For reference I am 5' 2" and 115lbs and usually wear a size 4. These run from small to x-large. I ordered the small and there was still a little room but it is supposed to be a relaxed fit. They are a good weight but not a heavy jean. They are a cropped fit and being a shorty it still ended up being slightly cropped so if you are a taller girlie it will crop even higher on your leg. They are very comfortable and have a slight stretch to them as well. All in all I am very happy with these pants.

Had a weird greasy feel to them, way too short on me, legs were different lengths. I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got this in a size L and they fit great around my waist, butt, and thighs. They were really short though, I know they are supposed to be cropped but they were ridiculously short on me. To add to that the two legs were shaped slightly differently and uneven, it was really odd.These were also really weird feeling. They had a greasy feel to them that left my hands feeling slippery, I ended up trying them on over leggings because I didn't want to get the residue on my legs. I have never had a pair of jeans have this kind of greasiness to them.I was really excited to try out these barrel leg jeans and very disappointed in the quality. Light flowy material My waist size is about 24-25 inches, hips are 39". The small fit me perfectly. The band is stretchy, of course, but it does not stretch too easily - it sits tight on me. I love the pockets too. Overall, nothing not to like about these pants!

MY FAVORITE PANTS I LOVE THESE! I'm 5'2" so buying pants online is always a little bit of a chance because usually they're too long but these are perfect! I'm also curvy so I usually don't wear straight leg "mom jean" type pants because they can make me look frumpy, but these are super flattering! I got them in September 2023 (6 months ago) and they are still in perfect condition, I've worn them ice skating, sledding in the snow and they're still in perfect condition. They're not very thick and feel thin so I was worried they'd rip when I totally failed at ice skating but they didn't. Because they're thin they don't get too hot or feel too thick, and I wore leggings under them in the snow and they worked great and I didn't get cold. They also have a lining on the inside so it feels like I'm wearing swishy pants. I wear them at work a lot with a blouse so they work for a business setting, and they're my go to coffee shop pants so they can be dressed down and up. I'm literally going to wear these until they die then buy another pair.

So comfy For size reference I am 5’2” 138lbs and a 32D. I purchased a size small. This set is SO cute! Perfect relaxing outfit.

Feels luxurious compared to other loungewear I’ve tried! I am at a point in my life where I want comfort and luxury or at least look and feel more luxurious! I have had 4 abdominal surgeries/Csections (5 kids) and just had a hysterectomy. I have been looking for clothes that make me feel good and look better than typical sweat outfits, etc. with a flattering effect or piece. I am pleasantly surprised by this outfit. It is a little see through for the white one, but I seriously would be surprised if it wasn’t see through (duh, it’s white) . Maybe it’s the white color that makes it look a little fancier? Not sure. But I do like it! The texture is soft (I’m a seam and texture type). I just tried it on and I’ll be sure to wear it for a day indoors! I’m a klutz in white! I’m 158lbs, 5’4” and one week post surgery (filled with air) - I think it’s fits nice now and I only expect it to fit even nicer once the bloat goes down.

Cute sizing is strange So they are super cute, comfy, and the colors are amazing. I do wear them regularly but they are definitely made for a certain body type. They aren't jean material like I thought they would be, they are stretchy yoga pant material. With that being said there are no pockets or belt loops. Let me start with telling y'all I'm 5ft 10inches and 142 pounds and ended up with the medium. If you are tall and thin like me you may run into some issues with sizing. The leg length to waist ratio wasn't too great. If I got the correct size in the waist (small) I needed 2 sizes up for length. But the size that fit my length (large) are huge in the waist. So I went in the middle and bought a medium. They are a little shorter than I would like and a little baggy in the waist. Since there is no belt loops there is not much I can do with that. So for us tall ladies there is definitely an issue in sizing.

My new yoga pants I absolutely love these!! They're very soft, stretchy, and not see through. I'm 5'10 and wear an American Eagle 12 Long but these pants are just a tad too long when I wear Vans. So I'd keep that in mind if you don't have long legs. The only other thing I'd note about these is that they have NO belly control. I take comfort in having even a little (even though I have a flat stomach) but if you have a belly or are just self conscious, I'd be a little weary. I'd also note that if you don't have hips, these pants may be difficult to stay on you. I get the impression that these slide down on others because they slide a little on me. Just got reference: I'm 5'10, 42 inch hips, 32 inch waist. American Eagle pant size 12 Long.

Certain Colors Are Made Differently I purchased two colors to see if either would work for a 70s party. Although the measurements and fabric on both pair are listed as being identical, the measurements and fabric were actually different. I’m 5’10 barefoot, weigh 185 lbs., and ordered a Large. Black Pair: The black pair were 2-3 inches shorter than the printed black-and-blue ones. They were highwaters on me without wearing shoes. The fabric was extremely thin, which is great for summer. They were also comfortable and stretchy, but they’re made like a super-thin, cheap t-shirt…or the cheapest black leggings you can find. A medium would’ve been fine, but I ordered Larges in hopes of having extra length. The black pair will pick up lint (etc.) fairly easy, they wrinkle fast and came out of the package SUPER wrinkled, and they’ll surely fade or shrink after a wash or two. Great purchase for the price if you only want a few wears out of them. Black-and-Blue Pair: The quality was much better. The black-and-blue pair had a little more “suction” and elasticity, they were just as comfortable, and they were much longer. I can wear them with heels and they’ll be long enough. The picture shows how they looked right out of the package, which is very different from the black pair. Hope this helps!

When I opened the package, the fabric felt buttery soft. The pants have details that set it apart from plain yoga pants. I’m 5’ 8” tall and found the length to be too long. I’m going to shorten them. These are not vanity sized. I’d say order up a size. A size chart would be . They are not completely resistant to cat fur. On the picture, you will see cat fur. It’s kind of curious that the pants have a label front and back on the inside of the waistband. I’m used to seeing that on the back only. I have one issue with these pants. The wide elastic is floating within the waistband and as such, it is prone to twisting. I’ve sewn active wear and it’s more common to have the elastic sewn down on one end to prevent this from happening. That’s annoying, but otherwise they fit well, drape well and are comfortable to wear. I appreciate the pockets for your phone etc.

Perfect high waisted dress pants! I purchased these pants to pair with a champagne colored crystal top I got for NYE last minute, and they ended up being perfect! Perfect flowy pants! I have tried on a lot of wide leg pants and these are my favorite. Very flowy material that drapes well. Comfortable, roomy, and elegant. I am 5’3 179 lbs with big tummy. Amazon recommended XXL. Technically it fit but was stylishly baggy, but too long unless I wear heels. I ordered M, L and XL to compare. Medium fit best because it was shorter length so I can wear it with flats. Can feel it is tighter at waist but still stretchy and comfortable. L feels like my correct size in waist but a little long, so am sticking with medium. L would be good with heels or I can hem them. I feel sizing is accurate overall since I am petite enough to wear size 8-10 M in oversized styles but tend to wear size 10-12 jeans or dresses. My recommendation is to choose your usual size if you are tall or wear heels. Size down if you are petite or short, because the style is so loose and flowy that it is better to get the length rightMy photos: XXL in khaki, M in brown and L in light blue

The only downside is the ties don’t always stay tied so if you are wearing them straight leg style, they can drag on the ground.

They wash well and the corners on the pockets don’t curl up and it is minimal wrinkling. I have washed them several times and I put them in the dryer with everything else. The size is true. If I were to translate it to a number size I would say it is a size 6. If you are over 5’5 they might be too short. I love the ties on the bottom to change the style from straight leg to more of a jogger. The only downside is the ties don’t always stay tied so if you are wearing them straight leg style, they can drag on the ground.

Great for Long Legs I ordered these for my 19 yr old who’s slim and tall - approx 5’8.5.” I was initially hesitant because the fiber content for these pants is 95% polyester. However, like a lot of other teens, she also wears oversized hoodies and sweatpants in our 80 plus degree weather. Her waist is about 28,” but per the brand’s size guide, I ordered these in XL - the largest size available. Not only is the material surprisingly light, they also fit her well. They’re a smidge loose in the waist, which is what she prefers and also makes for a comfier fit. We’re both super happy the length is perfect for her long legs. (That’s her modeling in the attached image) The quality seems great - she’s worn these several times without any issues. Per other reviews, apparently these aren’t for everyone. (I know I’m most comfortable in natural fibers). However, my daughter’s very happy and although I received these through Amazon Vine, at under $20, I’m definitely purchasing a backup pair for her to take back to college. The brand, Yoawdats, has a store here on Amazon with some other cute items she might like. Overall I do recommend these pants, but strongly suggest following the brand’s size guide.

I added a chain belt, and I love these pants I love the look and feel of these pants. They are super cute. I felt like I needed to break up the black though, so I added a gold chain belt. It actually made a huge difference, in my opinion. I guess you could achieve the same result by wearing a different color blouse. I wanted to wear the black blouse shown in my picture, so the belt helped break it up a little. The only change I would have liked to make in the pants was for them to be just a bit shorter. It might just be that I ordered the large and really needed the medium, but they seem to fit every place else. Looking at the size options, I now see that there is a short option. I might try that the next time around, even thought I considered "average" at 5'5", and not "short." Regardless, I really love these pants and can think of so many great ways to wear them.

Strange, long length Overall, I would say that these pants are average, which is why I am giving them an average 3-star rating. The main reason I'm not a huge fan of them is because they are a weird length. In the photos they look like a capri length, but they're much longer than that. I'm tall (5'11"), and they are as long as pants on me. So if you're taller they might be cropped/capri length, but if you're shorter they would probably be too long even for pants. The material is quite thin and see through. It doesn't have much stretch to it at all as it's almost like a chiffon type material, so I would definitely size up if you want them to fit flowy and loose as pictured. I sized up two from my regular size, and they fit well in the waist and legs. Finally, although it says they are ivory white in color, I would say they are more of a tan color. I don't mind the color though.

These are nice pants. I consider the fabric to be a linen, crepe like polyester that is comfortable to wear. The size is accurate according to given size chart. I often take a 3xl in bottoms, and I got a 3xl in these. They got loosely, as they are supposed to fit I believe. I suppose, it would be possible to fit into a smaller size if you are on lower end of size chart as these are generously sized. I am 5’8” and they come stop above my ankles, do a little cropped on me. The legs are wide and loose. I tend to get black in most pants, but got the gray in these, expecting more of a cotton like fabric. Although the gray color is accurate, I wish I had got black because I feel I could dress them up and get more wear. The elastic waistband on these is just right as far as comfort.

Nice color, lightweight, a bit genie-like I got these pants so I don't always need to wear shorts in the warm climate where I live. These pants are lightweight so I'm able to wear them outside even in warm temperatures. Thank goodness! I love the orange color! I can't wear it with everything, but that's okay. I've got plenty of jeans and black bottoms. The orange is something fun and different! The fit is pretty nice. I feel like a bit of a genie or gypsy in them. I think they look best with a tight-fitting top, although the right loose top would look pretty good as well. I just haven't found one I love with them yet. I feel like I have no butt in these, haha! But then again, I don't have much of a butt to begin with so that may be the problem. The pants come folded and wrapped up in a small round container. I thought that was pretty funny! I took pictures right after taking them out so they appear to be quite wrinkled in them. However, after a wash, they're much better. I didn't iron them and I don't have many wrinkles at all. I'm 5'9" and about 151 pounds and got a size large. I think that was the right call for a person of my size. I wish I had more of a defined waist, as I think these would look cuter. Overall, I'm happy with these lightweight, ankle length pants that add a fun flair to my wardrobe!

I've got plenty of jeans and black bottoms. The orange is something fun and different! The fit is pretty nice. I feel like a bit of a genie or gypsy in them. I think they look best with a tight-fitting top, although the right loose top would look pretty good as well. I just haven't found one I love with them yet. I feel like I have no butt in these, haha! But then again, I don't have much of a butt to begin with so that may be the problem. The pants come folded and wrapped up in a small round container. I thought that was pretty funny! I took pictures right after taking them out so they appear to be quite wrinkled in them. However, after a wash, they're much better. I didn't iron them and I don't have many wrinkles at all. I'm 5'9" and about 151 pounds and got a size large. I think that was the right call for a person of my size. I wish I had more of a defined waist, as I think these would look cuter. Overall, I'm happy with these lightweight, ankle length pants that add a fun flair to my wardrobe!

Easy, Comfortable I'm 5'3", 114 lbs and ordered the small. They fit as expected: since I'm short, they hit right at the ankle rather than a crop length. My sideview picture shows them riding a little lower on my hips, which still works. They're pretty comfortable and easy to throw on for lounging or taking walks. The pockets are a little too low to be practical, but they look cute. I ordered the dark blue, which in the product photos looks almost like denim, but it's not. In reality, it's a solid dark blue (more like the fabric closeup product photo). Oh and it came in a can, btw. Ha.

These are GORGEOUS pants! Just wish they were slightly longer! Worth the $! These are unlike any other pants that I own, which is a very good thing. My only gripe is that i wish they were a tad longer. This happens to me a lot, but being that I am only 5’6’’, I am not TALL, but it happens very frequently, so i don’t dry any of my pants, pretty much, in the dryer for this reason. ANYWAY, I like to be able to wear a small wedge or platform heel. These i can only wear with my sneaks. But it could be worse, they could be more cropped, so they are for sure wearable, but i’ll hang to dry. If you are taller, make note of this. I mean, I won’t give these lower than 5 stars because they’re perfect. Got that sheen, as i show in my pic. I will try to take a photo wearing them soon. I have no help at the moment to do so. They are thick enough (no worries about being see through), without being so thick that they weigh you down & are too heavy for the springtime. They’re PERFECT in weight, style, comfort- everything! They’re unique, fun & can be worn casually, or for a night on the town. I have no other pair like it, so that intrigued me. They have an elastic back, which i LOVE, and it makes it forgiving (size wise). No worries if you gain a few lbs. or have a bloated day. I could have even gone with an XS, if they had it, due to the stretch. {I’m 5’6’’, 129 lbs., 27.5 waist, 36 inch hips. Small fits, and has a bit of room to spare, so you may wish to size down on this one, due to the stretch.} If you’re tinier, the rise will just be lower. 5+ stars! I’d love other colors, as I am a bit too old for the hot pink one. I love the shade, but doubt I’ll be bold enough to wear pants that hue, but hey.. we’ll see. I never say never. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ ** I hope my review has been 😊👍🏻. **

Love these! These are gorgeous and very flattering. I wasn’t sure what size to get but I went with my instinct and got a small. Since they are in women sizing and I am usually a size 4-6. They stretch and fit very comfortable and the quality feels high end. For reference I am 145lbs, 28” waist 5’4.
The pants are amazing and well made. The colour is perfect for me. I am 133lbs and 5’2. Not too tight. Room to eat.

Actual denim fabric These pants are made of a nice thick denim fabric. No stretch to the fabric except in the waist. Good quality fabric and well made. They do run a little long. I’m 5’9” and they are about an inch longer than I’d like but everything else makes up for that.

Snazzy These pants are so fun and on trend. They could be dressed up or down I’m tall 5’11 approximately 175. I usually wear a 12/14 and first purchased an extra large, which was baggy in the legs- returned for a large. Additionally ordered the hot pink and silver. I’m returning the silver because they’re a bit more lavender than desired. The hot pink is scrumptious. These pants are a bit short for us tall gals but I can style with ankle strap shoes or boots. Nice fabric and good value.

Waistband is small! These pants are super adorable, and very nice quality. I absolutely LOVE the pink color. However, here are the issues I have with these pants: 1. The waistband is soooo tight. I stretched them out on the back of a chair for two days hoping it would make the elastic give a little. I even steamed it while stretched but neither gave me much, if any relief. 2. I am tall, 5'9" and pants are always short on me. I can make these long enough by pulling them down from my waist (which then makes the elastic even tighter when lowered to my hip) but I have a lot of sagging in the crotch. I will still wear these but will have to wear them with a long silk shirt that will cover up the crotch area. 3. Last things to know are that you should NOT wear any lotion of any kind when wearing these pants. I mistakenly had some on when I wanted to wear these and this fabric really shows moisture so I couldn't wear them and had to send them to the wash. Which, MAKE SURE YOU WASH THEM SEPARATELY. The pink dye in these pants bled onto other items in my wash. Nothing was important so I didn't care but everyone should know and take heed of this warning. Anyway, super cute pants. I may just take the elastic out completely (if that is possible) and make it a drawstring waist because I DO want to wear these.

Material is very comfortable A tad loose, but the material feels very comfortable and stretchy. The band comes up over the belly except in the back it only comes up mid back, which slides down and pulls it down in the front as well. The tightening loop inside the band is nice it helps it stay up better, but since they’re loose on me I had to tighten it pretty tight to get it to stay up, which gets uncomfortable for the top of my belly. The length is also a bit short, I have short legs and they come right under my ankles. Normally a size 6 and ordered a medium (6-8), I’m 5’3”, 153lbs and 21 weeks pregnant.

Comfortable. Nice buy. Fits larger calves well. Pros- Very comfortable and soft. Stay up all day! Fit my large calves. I’m glad I purchased these after shopping around. Cons- I’m 5’6” and they are bit too long. There were about 3 wrinkles around ankle, but nothing dramatic. I actually rolled top of sock over a little (see pic) and fit perfect with no wrinkles and no slippage. The most annoying is that the toe seam slips during the day and ends up partly on bottom. Black color is not opaque but is quite dark. You can see sheerness by looking at my toes in picture. Really like these and would purchase again.

The material seems really thin at first... like “can you see what color underwear I’m wearing” thin. However, they're not see through and don’t feel too fragile. I’m 5’0” and these were wayyyy too long for me. I cut probably 5-7 inches of length off the bottom and they are still just above the ground. They don’t have much stretch at all, but are still very comfortable. Even with those things considered, I love the fit and the style!!! I get so many compliment. They’re arguably my favorite pants. I just hope they hold up overtime.

Perfect Pants for the Holidays These tartan plaid pants are really cute! The perfect pant for Christmas celebrations. The red color is vibrant, and the pattern is really beautiful. The big bow that ties in the front adds festive style! I found them to be plenty long enough for my long legs (I normally wear 32 inch inseam jeans). They did run a touch snug though in the waist, so if you are between sizes in that area you may want to size up. There was a lot of extra room in the hip area though. I like the cut with the gathered waistline and loose, wide leg. Very on trend currently. The fabric was different than I expected. It is heavier and a bit more stiff than other dress pants in my closet. It feels like a quality fabric that should last. I do wish that there were pockets. For that missing element, I took off one star. All told, I am quite pleased with these fun red plaid pants. Looking forward to wearing them at the family holiday celebration!

Excellent pants with some caveats I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

Unexpectedly in love! So, let me start off by saying they don’t look as good in the pictures as I feel they do in real life. I am 5’9” and between 175-180 depending on the week of the month 🙄. Anyway! I LOVE these. It’s so hard for me to find pants that don’t give me camel toe because one of my hips is a little higher up than the other. But these pants fit so great. I thought they’d be a little longer, but I really don’t need to wear heels at all with these. I actually have to wear them a little lower than I wanted because they don’t go as long as I’d like - I wanted them to sweep the floor honestly. But if that’s not your style then you might want heels and you can wear them comfortably high on your waist. So I’d say, definitely size up bc I’m usually a large in pants and also definitely buy these pants !

Run a bit big but look fine with maternity shirt over the baggy space Currently 21 weeks pregnant, 5' 9", 145#. this is my second baby so my belly is a lot bigger this time around and I needed maternity scrubs. I ordered these in medium (my pre-pregnancy Wonderwink size) and the Cherokee maternity scrubs in a small. I found that the Cherokee maternity scrubs were very well fitted except for the fact that the belly band was way too tight and by the time I was nine months they would be impossible to wear. I have the same problem with these scrubs as other reviewers that the space in between the tummy and your legs is very big, however, once I put them on with a maternity shirt you can't tell the difference anyway. The good thing about them being a little big is I definitely have room to grow and they are super comfortable. Far more comfortable than the Cherokee were. I was going to try them in a small but I ultimately decided that the medium would fit me the best all the way through my pregnancy.

Thank you reviews that recommended sizing down First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

Buyer Beware: Poor Fabric Quality in Samantha I purchased these jeans in 3 colors: Dark Blue (8/13/21), Quinn (2/15/22), and Samantha (4/24/22). All 3 were in size 5 and fit great (I'm about 5'5" 120 pounds). Dark Blue has a smoother fabric that’s not quite denim and while the leg cut is the same style, the back pockets are different, they’re more trouser looking. Quinn and Samantha are the same style, both have the “W” logo stitching in the back pockets. Quinn was made of thicker/sturdier denim and cost $50 but Samantha cost $69 and is in a thinner cheaper denim. Samantha being the newest one barely lasted 3 months because there's a rip along the back pocket. Thankfully I noticed the tear while folding laundry and was not out in public. Each pair isn’t cheap but for Samantha to cost the most at $69.00, I would expect it to last way more than 3 months!

A Review from a girl who Actually Collects These Styles of Pants Hi! So okay, I have had a few pairs of these “soft, boho pattern flare pants” throughout my young life. I remember when they peaked back in early 2010s! So, if you see my photos, I already owned a few pairs in Red/Pink and Black/White. These pants are my favorite. They are so soft, such good quality. They feel like cotton, they’re pretty thick (and don’t show panty lines!), and they’re the perfect length on my 5’7 self, they are truly the best. I really wanted some in Blue, but I couldn’t find any in my area. So I decided to give Amazon a shot. I’m a bit disappointed. You may not be able to tell from my photos, but these pants are… different. The pattern is clearly laser printed. It doesn’t look as ‘real’ as the red and black ones. Also, the quality is much worse. It doesn’t feel like soft cotton, it feels more like polyester. The pattern does look good, but it’s just not the same. The fit is fine, but it’s more lightweight than my genuine boho pants. It’s also more see through!?! That’s not supposed to happen, IMO. Overall, they’re thinner, not as soft, and really cheaply made. But still, If you’re just going for a boho look, then go for it. I’d say, if you can, try to find the real deal boho flares in discount department stores.
