Slim fit, great pockets I expected these to fit like sweatpants. In the front they kind of do but in the back, they have a very slim fit. I thought this made them hang really nice, mire like dress slacks, but it might be challenging for someone who is bigger through the hips and buttocks region. Despite being a knit fabric there’s not a whole lot of stretch to the material. The material is very soft and comfortable. The pockets are amazing. There are six packets including two cargo on the sides small pockets on the back and deep pockets for your hands upfront. I washed these and they didn’t have any color fade or pilling. Seem to be wearing well. For the right figure, I think these are an excellent find. They were long enough for my 5 foot seven frame, and it would be ideal for someone who is slimmer through the hips and butt, frustrating if you’re bigger in those areas. A little poochy in the front like a sweat pant but overall very nice. I think they will be a comfortable option for travel- look nicer than sweatpants, have pockets for storage, and will be comfortable.

Confy & Stylish But Is Sheer =[ These are one of the most comfiest palazzos I have every purchased. They are soft, made out of polyester, have some elasticity, in addition to the waistline, and to top it off the ribbon bow tie, too cute! You can definitely pair these with a cute tank, or t-shirt when either heading over to the beach or just to spend a day out with some friends. They can even be worn to the office. Myself being 5'2, the pants are long on me so I will have to get them altered knowing that I purchased high-rise palazzo. I have nothing else negative to say about the pants minus the fact that they are sheer, especially if you hold them up against the window. When pairing these pants to an outfit I fell you can still wear them, but wear a longer cardigan to desired length to prevent anything being seen. I dislike it when my clothes are sheer, and I prefer to wear either something underneath or over to cover them. Don't wear anything thick because these pants are to be worn in comfortable and warm weather. After all, I leave it to the buyer however they wish to wear them, the can. Overall great product in terms of appearance, quality and durability!

Perfect Jeans for Texas cold weather Was afraid the fleece would be too much and make the jeans fill stiff and hard to move in. I’ve worn them all day chasing around a one year old and they have been amazing. I bought “Yehopere Women's Winter Fleece Lined Jeans Slim Fit Warm Skinny High Waist Denim Jean” size M. I normally wear size 6-8 in jeans. I’m 5’7, weigh 150-165 pounds. I’m not skin and bones. I wouldn’t call myself curvy but as I said I did have a baby a year ago and I’m surprised how by how much I really do love these jeans. For the price, they’re a must for Friday night football games sitting on cold metal. The back pockets are also fleece lined and the pair I bought has all working pockets, none are. Fabric really does appear and feel like denim on the outside. There is a good bit of stretch to them as well.

Great quality, runs small These jeans look great and are really cozy -- I love them! The lining is soft and very warm, and the fabric has good stretch. The fit is medium-high waisted and surprisingly skinny for jeans with a thick lining. Judging from other reviews, I'm guessing sizing varies depending on color and style. In light blue, they definitely run small. I'm 5'3" 135 lbs and usually take a medium, but I ordered large because I wanted them to be comfortable and to fit over a base layer in extreme cold. Good thing I did -- the large fits perfectly, with no room for a base layer (fortunately, I won't need one, because the lining is plenty warm). The bottom hits my foot, so if you're any shorter than me, you'll need to cuff them. These are going to be my favorite winter jeans for spending time outdoors.

Not the Palazzo Pants I was expecting Pictured: Front and back of the pants before and after wash. To me, the term "Palazzo pants" evokes a longer, more flowy silhouette. The length feels more akin to long gauchos or very baggy capris. The inseam on these pants are 24" so bear that in mind when you purchase. They must have used models that are maybe 5' 5" or 5' 6" for the hem to almost hit the ground. Or maybe the photos are edited. I got these pants thinking they'd at least hit my ankles or skim the top of my feet. No such luck. The cotton fabric feels like light denim or twill. It's kinda stiff, so the pantlegs retain their voluminous shape. The contrasting white stitching on the seams give these pants a very casual look. I'm not 100% sold on the wide white string tie. I may remove it altogether since the waistband is elasticized. Be sure to air dry as these pants are cotton and will shrink up even higher!

100% thick, quality cotton, wide leg pants Surprisingly, length wise, they fit me super well. I am 5‘4“, 110 lbs. I ordered the size XS. I don’t have to flip the waist over, as i normally do with pants. I can wear them as sewn and they graze the top of my foot. The waist is a little too snug to be comfortable though. That’s sad because in all other ways these pants are perfect. I wore them for several hours and was uncomfortable. The legs are wide. The ecru, bone color I chose looks so smart with a sweater. I wish they fit me better because they are cute. . The cotton isn’t blended, it’s actually 100% cotton, which I prefer. It’s thick enough to feel almost like twill, but soft and it drapes well. It’s a great weight for pants. Based on my experience, I would order up a size.

Comfy lightweight casual I live in a neutral wardrobe and I like these comfy courduroy pants. They're lightweight so they're a nice transitional piece between seasons. Elastic waistband and big pockets - it does seem to run pretty roomy. For reference, I'm 5'8" and 155lbs and ordered a medium. If I had a lot in my pockets these pants would be ridin' super low. :D

Finally, the Perfect Inseam! First and foremost, what I love most about these corduroy pants is the inseam. Too often, pants are manufactured with a 28 - 30 inch inseam, which would be TOO SHORT for the average woman (with a woman's curves). I ordered a size Small; I'm 5'5", 140 lbs and any pants I'd wear would need to be AT LEAST 32" to avoid being floods, particularly if wearing heels. These are the perfect length and I couldn't be more pleased. Great job to the manufacturers. 5 stars.

Odd fit The pants look great when I wear them with a long top, as in the product listing. But the fit under the top is odd and somewhat uncomfortable, mostly in the way the fabric bags out and folds down at the crotch. If I try to pull them up higher to prevent this, they come way above my waist. The pockets are smaller than I usually find in pants of this style. I can slide in my phone (I have a small one), but the top sticks out, making me worry that it might fall out. Also, as noted in the listing, these pants are 100% polyester. I hoped they might turn out to have at least a small amount of cotton, but they are in fact entirely synthetic fiber. The one thing I really like is the brown color, which is a kind of a rich copper shade. For reference, I'm 5'4" and 155 lbs, and I ordered size Medium. I was able to machine wash these in cool water and dry them on low heat without having them change their size or shape.

GET THESE PANTS NOW I was apprehensive about getting these pants because I thought they might not be flattering on me, but YEOOOOO I wear these boys like TWICE A WEEK IM IN LOVE. They're cute asf with a crop top or oversized sweater tucked in!!! And theyre so COMFORTABLE. The waist is completely elastic like pajama pants, but still has a button and zipper to look like jeans. I usually wear them with a belt to sinch in my waist a little more, but they're cute without one too! The pockets are DEEP too I can actually fit my phone in em its wild. I get compliments literally every single time I wear them! The only thing I could say is if you're on the taller side like me (5'10/11) the leg holes kind of ride up if you sit with your legs crossed or bend over. Just wear long socks or tights underneath if its cold, otherwise it really doesnt even matter. I think I got a 3xl just because I was scared they were Asian sizes and I wear a size 14/16 jeans so u know how it is lol. They fit GREAT but I could have comfortably gone down to a 2x or maybe even 1x. They really really feel like you're just wearing pajamas I love it

Comfortable As Jammies! These pants are so comfortable, I've just purchased two more pairs in different colors. I LOVE wearing these! I'm 5' 7", about 125-130 lbs, with a 29.5" natural (high) waist. The corduroy seems to be a slightly finer wale than is pictured? But it looks great, and is so COZY. I tried them on to photograph them, and then couldn't bring myself to take them off to switch for jeans. I wore them for the rest of the day. Walked the dogs in them, and though they're lightweight, I stayed warm. These are perfect for Hobbit Girl Autumn, and I am here for it. :) The pants look nice rolled up, or not. They have an *almost* barrel shape that is so in fashion right now, but they don't make you look like Popeye. More of a Lagenlook style. They're super cute with booties. I have a 2" wide belt, and it works perfectly with them.

Weird fit Even when I saw that these Cropped Corduroy Pants were 100% polyester I was not dissuaded from ordering them. This is because they are from Yesno, a brand that I own pieces from and wear often. If I didn't know that these were polyester pants, I wouldn't know. The fabric feels nice and soft, if pressed I would have guessed that they are a cotton blend. As is usual with this brand, these pants are very well made. They have roomy slash pockets in front and an interesting dropped yolk detail at the back waistband. The belt loops are nice and large so that a wide belt can be worn with them. The waist is mostly elastic and very stretchy, and the zipper and buttons at the front are nice quality. I'm very happy with the way these pants are constructed, the feel of the fabric and the rustic brown color. Sadly, for all I love about these pants they do not fit properly. They fit me, just not properly. There is a dropped crotch which was not mentioned in the listing. So if I have the pants fastened at my belly button or just above, the crotch is hanging two or three inches below that. This makes the rear end look weird because it puckers horizontally by the back seam. They do look better if I pull them up, but then the waist is right under my bra line. Even though a dropped crotch is not my preference, I would still wear them except the fit is not comfortable either. The back seam just rests against my hiney and somehow feels tight... Which is very strange since I'm pretty flat back there. So, even though the fit is not working for me these pants will probably work for others and I think are not a bad deal at $34.99. If you have a longer torso and more booty, these might be your pants! For reference, I am 5'8" 190 lb, and ordered the XL.

The picture is deceiving Couldn’t use too small my 13 year old granddaughter couldn’t wear them very disappointed shouldn’t be able to advertise as plus size waste of time and money I needed them for a special occasion so they did arrive on time be were a great disappointment when I couldn’t pull them up past my ankles and my ankles are normal size the picture of the item is very misleading the top band on the item prevents you from pulling them up it is so small please note I am very displeased and disappointed hop know one else has this problem I am checking to see if I can return them since no one I know can fit them comfortably

Please read this review. THE GOOD: —Nicely packaged. —Hose are sheer —Husband said they looked good —Elastic top —Reinforced toe —Top will stretch to 9” (with effort) —They were appropriate length (to my knee) —Colors were as expected Sounds good so far!! But… THE BAD: —Elastic top is REALLY snug —Uncomfortable after 20 minutes wear —After 35 minutes, I had to take them off because of the pain around the top. Felt like a rubber band around my leg. I’m 5’6” and weigh 194. I actually thought these knee highs would be too large, if anything. Nope! They are definitely not plus size. Remember, the gals in the advertising look like they’re happy as can be. I’ll bet in reality, they couldn’t wait to get done with the shoot so they could get circulation back in their lower legs. Bahaha!! Unfortunately I cannot in good conscience recommend these knee highs to plus size gals. They may be great, however for smaller ladies.

Comfortable but some issues. I am 5'9", 165lb. Ordered size large. These pants are comfortable and fit pretty well. They aren't as thin or as stiff as linen, but still breathe. I also appreciate the deep pockets. However, there are some things I don't like about them. My major complaint is the light-colored material is see-through. I hate that I will have to consider what type of undies or shirt I wear. Also, the front is somewhat baggy while the back seems to cling and emphasizes my crack when bending forward. The material also clings to show the front pockets underneath. Finally, the back pockets are stitched more to the side than the back. What is frustrating about this is that if they were stitched to the back, they could have helped with the transparency issue! I will keep them for comfort, but not order more.

These cotton work pants are a nice weight cotton fabric, and very comfortable. They do require a little ironing after washing and drying because they do wrinkle some. Being 97% cotton, the pants did shrink when washed on cool and dried on light. Inside seams are straight going down pantleg. Two front and two back pockets hold my cell phone without falling out. The drawstring is a nice addition if you need to tighten the pants. The elastic around the waist doesn't bunch or irritate my skin. The length is perfect for a 5' 2 height.

Size chart is body measurements not garment measurements These khaki pants are made of nice quality, thin cotton fabric, with front and rear pockets large enough to hold a cell phone (yay!). The size chart is true to body (not garment) measurements, so these size large pants are too big to fit my daughter's 41" hips. The pants are very comfortable, but they aren't really suitable for yoga. These are casual, elastic-waist pants that would be great for someone who has trouble with zippers, since they are easy to put on and take off.

PERFECT inseam but waste size is tricky As one of the shorter ladies, I’m soooooo thankful to have found these pants in just the right length. I’m 4’11 and 108 lbs but have the dreaded mommy tummy. The black pants are a very forgiving material. I ordered an XS and it’s working fine. I loved them so much I ordered the charcoal but the material is not as forgiving so I’m returning to try for a medium.

Good but not great For reference, I ordered an XL/31" inseam based on the sizing chart - I can't confirm if they're actually XL since the tag looks like it was chewed away or something. The pants are very comfortable and fit well, they are just not made that well. As others have noted, they're covered in lint/hair, so keep a lint roller handy. I loved that this has front and back pockets, but the front pockets weren't sewn properly so there are holes in them. I can have my phone or something big in the right pocket, since that hole isn't too big, but I wouldn't risk the left since my whole hand fit through it. I would've sent them back but I needed them for the next day, so I made it work. If they were made better, I'd definitely order another pair, but I don't know if I'd want to risk it.

Best Work Pants EVER!!! I don't normally leave reviews, but I absolutely had to for these pants. I am a server at a very busy restaurant, in Florida where it gets hot as heck. I was out of work for 2 months thanks to covid shutting down all our restaurants, and well I gained some weight while sitting at home doing nothing 🤣🤣🤣. I was called to return to work on Memorial Day weekend and of course, my black work pants would not fit over my newly found, voluptuous body. I spent 2 days running from store to store, fitting rooms were closed and I was getting desperate. I bought and returned no less than 10 pairs to various stores. I found 2 pairs that fit decently, but I absolutely hated because they didn't fit right. I have a small waist, big hips, big thighs, etc., so I need more of a stretchy material. I gave in and hit Amazon figuring if I had to buy and return multiple pairs, I could at least do it in the comfort of my own home. Well, this was the 1st pair i ordered. I can absolutely say, i LOVE LOVE these! They fit perfect, stretch in all the right places, they are light enough so they aren't hot, so soft, unlike starchy, rough dress pants. If you have a job where you are required to wear black dress pants, I highly recommend these. For reference, sizing is spot on. I am 5'7", 165 lbs and typically wear a size 11 juniors/8 or 10 womens. I bought the Medium 31" inseam and they fit perfectly! I ordered 2 more pairs right away!

Awesome pants It took me forever to decide on finally buying these, but I am so glad I did. I'm not the skinniest gal around, but I find these to be generally flattering for most body types. Most of my weight is around my middle and I'm currently working out 4 days a week and watching carbs to cut this down...but in the meantime, I needed comfortable work pants for my bartending job. These pass the squat test, and! The bend and snap in case you're trying to impress anyone. I don't know how they hold as I've only had them a week, but I'm super happy with how stretchy and comfy they are, and that I'm not constantly fussing with a zipper or yanking my pants up. I am 5'8 on a good day and 188 pounds and dropping, and bought the XL/33". I actually feel these are an inch or so too long for me, but I fold them up so they don't drag. I also feel I could've gone with a large and plan on my next pair being a L/31" as I continue to lose weight. ***Updating*** These lasted a good while and I still have them, but found I do definitely prefer the more expensive Betabrand Pants. I'm now 174 and the XL are too baggy. I did end up ordering a L/31 & they fit better.

These are a must! I'm terrible at taking pics. But I got some of the pants, the length, and with an entire outfit. I hope they help. For reference, I am 5'0" and a out 130lbs (changes between 128-133 often, because well... food) anyway..... If you have any type of belly weight, I would recommend sizing up. My belly weight is just skin from losing a lot of weight (I was 165 last year, but being this short created a lot of extra skin in my fupa area). If you have extreme belly weight, these can still work for you. Just size up and wear a longer shirt. The pants are lovely. They have a great stretch and they help tuck in and hide a few problem areas for me. The length is perfect. I got a size small with 27" inseam. I have these in black and khaki now. I will absolutely be buying a few more in different colors and may go up a size on a few for a more relaxed fit. (Ya know.. some days bloating takes over and I just want it to hang a little more loose). If you're on the fence, I would recommend taking a leap of faith. You can dress them up and down. I mean, I wouldn't exactly wear them to a yoga class, but they could certainly handle the job if you wanted to try. I'm wearing them at work right now. Hence the bathroom pics b3cause I don't have full length mirrors at home.

They are slim through the hips, which works for me but just something to be aware of as the elastic is only in the back. The only reason I gave them 4-stars instead of 5 is the fit isn't very flattering on a short person (as you can see from the photos :))

They FIT!! I’ve seen these adorable pants everywhere on Amazon but never in my size. I’m usually a 12/14 or XL. I also prefer all cotton/natural fibers; it’s very hot & humid here in Fl. I decided to take a chance on these in size Large and happy to , not only do they fit, but they fit well! I carry excess weight in my tummy. My waist is approx 36/37” and my hips are 43.” I prefer my clothes to fit oversized and loose. Think Free People, which these btw, are pretty good dupes. The length is spot on for me at 5’5” and 165lbs. I’m wearing 2.5” platforms on my images. The only reason I took one star off is for the fiber content. I was surprised when I saw the care tag read 100% cotton. They def feel like they contain some type of synthetic material mixed in with cotton. They did soften up after washing on low with fabric softener. I also dried on low and there was zero shrinkage or fading of colors. Despite my mixed feeling about the material, these are super lightweight and comfortable. The elasticized waistline is stitched nicely in place so no worries about it twisting or turning. The drawstring is also functional, not just decorative and the front (and back) pockets are a nice touch. I received these through Amazon Vine, but I’d pay full for these. However, I probably wouldn’t wear these in the heat of summer. These will be great to throw on with a cotton tee or tank and a cardigan as it gets cooler. My only other issue is what color to get next - love the great variety of brilliant shades from which to choose. The brand name, Ziesexy, makes me chuckle, but I do recommend them. I’ve purchased some really cute cotton tops from them with which I’m super happy. Definitely recommend these pants and the brand which has a store here on Amazon.

Reviewed by spouse: Absolutely love these! These feel like a cotton/linen blend and look more expensive and nice in person than I was expecting. I had previously ordered another pair and when they got here, they were 100% polyester and were awful…the colors, the pattern and the material…Yuck. These are a total winner! Yes, these look just like the FP version. They do run a little bit long and large, so I would say to size down if anything. I am wearing a small and for sizing reference, I’m 5’3 & 132lbs and they are several inches too long and have could probably be 15 to 20 pounds heavier and still fit in the small. They do not have any stretch, but have pockets. Instead of hemming them, I’m just going to wear them with a wedge. These are just some universal threads espadrille sandals (maybe 2 inch heels…included a picture) so you could see and these make the length absolutely perfect for me. These are perfect for both summer & fall due to color and fabric.

Unexpected hit. Tag reads 100% cotton I was surprised to like these striped, cotton drawstring pants as much as i do. They are cute with the stripes and pockets (in the front and back). The description says they are a blend of cotton but the tag reads 100% cotton. They do feel stiffer than most cotton garments I’m used to. This doesn’t bother me for pants. They actually got a little stiffer after washing and machine drying. There didn’t seem to be a noticeable difference in any shrinkage. I am 5‘4“. 110 lbs and I got the small. The waist is just right on me. I wouldn’t want it any tighter. The length is good. They hit the top of my foot. I love the pink amd brown coloring. It matches all neutrals and whites. It’s a fun casual pant that flatters my shape.

Decent tracksuit: but has some flaws This 2 piece tracksuit is decent for the price. It runs very true to size - I ordered XL: I'm 5'10" and around 150lbs. It's actually a little bigger than I prefer: but I'd rather order a size up than have it be too small. The fabric has a lot of stretch to it and can accommodate varying bust/hip sizes. It does has some flaws though, which may be a deal breaker for some people. The sleeves are too short - and the pants are a little too long + slightly baggy around the ankles. Also, the collar circumference of the top is way too big around my neck: even when it's fully zipped. To camouflage this, I plan on wearing it with the "lapels" turned down. The fabric is also a little bit scratchy feeling and not very soft. The white zippers are pure snow white color, while the white stripes of the fabric are an off-white color. You can tell if you really look in person but it's barely noticeable. Overall, this isn't too bad for the value if you can overlook some potential flaws. I give this 3 out of 5 stars

In terms of size, I would say that they are true to size and they also have a lot of stretch to them. On me, they're tight on the upper legs like yoga pants, and wide leg below the knee. They're also long enough on me, which is nice because I'm tall (5'11"). Only taking off one star because it says they are dressy pants, but they certainly are not. They are like sweatpants. So describing them as lounge pants would certainly be more accurate. I was hoping to wear these out, but I would only wear them around the house.

Love these super cosy, lightweight and stretchy Palazzo pants! Love, love these soft and stretchy pants! They have a forgiving fit that is very comfortable. The pants are lightweight but will provide a some extra warmth when it is needed on cool fall evenings. They have a high waist, but that is something I like with my apple shaped middle. The length is perfect for my 5' 4" frame so I am not using the bottoms as floor sweeps, nor will I have to dig out the platform shoes, lol. The material is very soft and feels so nice on my skin, both inside and outside. I love the Boho designs. The product page listed these as Boho red, but when I got them, I would say the red is more of a dark orange. Which of course, is perfect for fall! These are not just wear at home pants for me ... I wear this type of pants when I go shopping quite often. I am always happy to add another pair of them to my collection! I can definitely recommend these pants.

I love these pants!!! I'm a sucker for silver bling! These are true to size. I usually wear an XL (5'8", 205lb) but I sized down to a Large since I'm losing weight, and they fit as expected. They fit now thanks to the very stretchy material, but will be perfect and flowy once I get down to around 190. The material is a finely pleated metallic polyester. The face is very shiny, and the back is black. They have a wide elastic waistband that's quite comfortable. They're a tiny bit short on me right now but should relax down a couple of inches as I get closer to a true large size. These are flashy and fun, and great for either a night out or just around the house!

Below-average quality, short, price is too high In my opinion, these pants are below average. The fabric is not that great. It is medium-weight and has some stretch to it. It wrinkles but not too bad. The pants have two front and two back pockets. The waistband has elastic in it that is quite stretchy, but the pants also have a zipper fly with two buttons. There is also a piece of Velcro on the waistband in place of a hook or the inner button. I am not a fan of Velcro on my clothing except for some jackets. It tends to get caught on clothes and sometimes ruin them. For that reason, I would not be able to wear a silk blouse or a delicate sweater with these pants. I got these pants in medium size. (For reference, I am 5 feet 5 inches tall and weigh 125 pounds. I usually wear small to medium size clothing.) They fit me quite loose in the hip area, but those fuller than me in the backside should not have this problem. My main issue with these pants is the length. The product description states that the inseam is 27 inches. I measured mine, and it came to 26 inches. They are a bit too short for me, and there is not enough fabric in the hem to let out. I also believe that the price for these pants is quite high. The fabric looks and feels cheap, and the craftsmanship is not that great either. One of the belt loops on my pants has a long tail. So, overall, these pants are okay but not great.

Run small, nice thicker fabric and decent pockets. I wasn't sure what to expect from these but I have been looking for a nice midweight wide leg pant for work and thought I would give these a try.I really like the fabric, it is a good midweight that I can wear in the winter without freezing to death. The fabric has a sleek feel to it and the texture is somewhere between a jean and a knit pant, it's a very unique fabric. The fabric moves well and feels great on, it also doesn't seem to attract pet hair which I love since I have cats and always struggle with keeping black pants pet hair free.Unfortunately, these do seem to run a bit small. I am 5'7" and 155lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got these in a size L which says it has a 32-33" waist because I really don't like my waistbands tight. As you can see this waistband was pretty tight, not uncomfortably so, but I definitely have a bit of belly bulge going on here. The waistband has enough stretch that when I sit down it's still comfortable but not the most flattering.There are functioning pockets on the front and back. They are slightly smaller than normal pants pockets but still work to fit my wallet or phone (I have a Z4 fold).These pants are comfy and look cute if I have a shirt on that doesn't show the waistband. I really do like them but wish the waistband was an inch wider. I also think they are a bit pricey for what you are getting. However, overall I would recommend...just size up and don't go by their size chart.

FANTASTIC!! My husband has a medical condition that makes him feel very cold all the time. It’s hard to find reasonably-priced lined pants, so when I came across these on sale $10 off, I decided to try them. He loves them! They are thick and warm! Great quality. The first thing he did when he put them on was to put his (very large) cell phone in one pocket and his hard-cased reading glasses in the other pocket — both fit very nicely without making the pants too tight. He is 5’9” and weighs about 160 pounds. I got him size large—the ones with the cuff around the ankles because I didn’t want to hem them. These are great pants and I highly recommend them.I write honest reviews to help others make informed purchases because I read reviews myself and appreciate how they can be. I hope that my review helped you!

To all my fellow plus-sized ladies, just throw your old tactical pants in the trash and get these! Seriously. I am a size 16-18; I ordered both the size 18L and the 20R (a size up as other reviewers suggested), and I am so glad I did. The 18L were a little tight and the length was a little long (I am 5'9"-- but they were still wearable with my work boots)... and by tight, I mean I had to lay down on the bed and grab the channel locks. The 20R are prefect. They are super freaking comfortable and unbelievably flattering. I have a mommy pouch, and all other tactical pants made me super self concious about it; in fact, I think they accentuated it. I don't know what it is about the front panel of the pant, but it completely flattens your tummy. I also have a healthy sized rear end and thighs. They hug and flatter every curve, instead of giving you a saggy diaper looking butt or baggy pant legs like others. My hubby actually checked me out in work my work pants 😉 Spend the money on these; you won't regret it. These are super comfy, stretchy pants that look amazing and give me a great range of motion at work. With that said, they would be great for police, EMTs, and etc.

Finally I look like a girl in my uniform!! I am used to wearing unattractive men's prison pants as the pants for my Ranger uniform. I hate them. They are unflattering, but free. I've finally decided to get my own and these are so flattering! I have a butt! I look like a woman! So exciting! The fit.... I am 5'5'' and 155+lbs with a little extra weight in the middle. I got the 8 Long. The 8's fit nicely. Not tight to my body, but not loose. They were too long, which was expected. They did not have regular. I hemmed them myself no problem though. I recently lost some weight and am down to 147 lbs (and going!) and am wishing I got a size 6. They might fit a bit better. The style and function... They are higher waisted, hitting a couple inches below my belly button. The pockets are super useful- the thigh pockets have slots inside so you can organize what you put there. There is a smaller pocket in the front that I keep my chapstick in. I attach my radio to the main pockets on the front/sidd. The belt loops are thick and secure. I attached quite a few pictures: some showing the non-hemmed version, a couple hemmed.

Always my go to. If you’re thinking about buying these... just DO IT! These are the most comfortable tac pants I’ve owned. They fit like your favorite pair of jeans, have durable belt loops, and great pockets. They also have just the right amount of stretch so no guest appearances ;) the blue matches my uniform perfectly. They do fade slightly, BUT it’s after at least 50 washes, and fade about as quickly as the rest of my uniform. I’m 5’8” and Wore a 14 long when I was 185-200lbs, and have been wearing a 12 long from 165(current) to 185, although I may opt for the 10 long next time I buy. I’d say go one size up from the size of jeans you wear. If you’re over 5’8” I definitely recommend getting a long inseam because the regular inseam hits right above the ankle bone.

Not bad! Overall I like these and will be wearing them a lot. I purchased a size small and they fit me perfectly. I’m 5’3” 127 lbs. with a 27” waist. Casual Elegance, Light & Comfy These Lace Linen Pants are a big hit with my wife. She loves how they look and feel. They're light and comfy and perfect for Florida weather. Cute Fall Pant I'm 5'7 185lbs and thought I ordered my usual size large, but XXL was delivered instead. They are massive on me, so large probably would've fit just right. The material is very soft and I love the khaki and blue color combination. Hopefully they let me exchange these for the right size.

Wonderful dupe, excellent quality These pants are great! After seeming them all over the place from various influencers, I decided to get these and try them out. I had low expectations, expecting them to be that papery, stiff, cheap material… but I was pleasantly surprised. They’re so soft and feel like soft cotton. The colors are also not too bright (my picture makes them look a tad brigjr) and not too dull—-the perfect in between. They can be dressed up or down. It’s a great piece for walking the line of looking pulled together but also super comfortable, could be used as pajamas, but with the right shirt and shoes paired it can be dressed up. I wore them already and got several compliments. I am 5’7” and they were plenty long enough for me, exactly how I want it. They are flowy and not at all sheer. The waist does go up higher on me, closer to my belly button, but it looks just fine if it’s lower too. It’s got plenty of stretch and has a working tied waist. The pockets are also functional (both in the front and the back). That being said, I ordered my normal size medium and they fit great. I’m going to try to wear them as much as I can before summer is over!

So, so cute!!! Exceeded my expectations. So soft cotton. Thicker than expected, but super drapey and very comfortable. Purchased a large, probably should have ordered a medium, but I feel like the difference in fit wouldn't be significant enough to exchange them. I'm 5'4", 155 ish. They are long on me, but still look adorable. In one of the more muted colors I'd wear to work with a bootie, a tank and a jacket, they're that good.

These are LONG I'm 5'5", and these are really long on me. Aside from that I like them. The fabric is soft, and there are front and back pockets. The front pockets are straight so not really comfortable to keep your hands in but fine for placing things. I like that the back pocket is perfect for a cell phone. They are baggy, but meant to be baggy. With the drawstring, you can wear these at various sizes so if you fluctuate a lot or have just had a baby, these would be great. I think they're cute and casual for home or relaxing outings.

Too nice to wear to bed These pajama pants are meant to be seen. Pleasantly surprised these felt like high end cotton instead of a typical poly blend. Not sheer at all. Not heavy to wear. The bad is comfortable and not constricting even on my bloated days. Length is just slightly past my ankles so I can wear sneakers or even platforms and the length will work. Looks great with a sweater just like the model pictures but also paired well my cropped old navy tees and even with my body suits since these are dressy for the style type. Flattering, comfy, on trend wide leg striped pants! I am always on the lookout for a cute pair of pants that are comfy, yet stylish. I wear sweatpants often, and it is nice to have a more stylish alternative that is also just as comfortable to wear. These pants check all the boxes! They have an elastic waist with a fabric tie, a flowy wide leg cut, and a cute striped pattern. These pants are perfect to wear on casual days out shopping. I also love pants like this when I’m traveling or catching a flight. Another option I’m thinking of is wearing these pants as a bathing suit coverup in the summer with a fitted tank top or tee. The options are endless with these pants. I have worn similar pairs of pants with a fitted top and a denim jacket for a put-together look. I’m only 5’4 so the pants were a bit long on me and I wore them with platform sandals for the photo. But luckily- I actually like my pants to fit a little bit longer and drag the floor so I don’t mind! I would like to order these pants in several other colors because I can see myself living in them. 5 stars!

10 stars for these pants! These pants blew me away. I was not expecting them to be this great based on the pics the seller has provided. But they are! They are slightly longer than I’d like, but with these kitten heeled boots on, they look just right. They are very comfortable and have pockets, so cmon, that’s a win in itself for women’s clothing. I especially like that they’re not the usual white/beige color. The white is actually more of a cream and the beige is a greenish tan color. Very nice. I plan to wear the hell outta these pants in the winter and summer. Can be styled in so many ways bringing great value to my closet with a really affordable pair of pants.

Best affordable compression for curvy figure If you have the problem of leggings constantly falling off of your waist, these might be your lifesaver. These offer a lot of compression and can stretch high up on your waist so nothing jiggles. They are very smooth and silky like a second skin. They cinch in more at the waist and then fan out at the hips to flatter a curvier body but also stay put for all of the miles. They are squat proof and hold up well over many washes and wears (mine have gone over 1,000 miles and still seem like they have a bunch of life left in them). But they are also cool enough to wear in hot climates for more compression or sun protection. I have run several marathons and ultras in Texas and Florida in them. I also love that they can stretch over my ankles and offer more solid compression when I need it while traveling. This is a rare find as I’m nearly 6 ft tall. and mostly leg. I am not sure how this will affect more petite ladies, though, so something to keep in mind. My only complaint is that the shape/seams in the rear could allow more room and be more flattering for larger booties. But for the price and functionality, these are real winners.

Great material, very well made! Update: This memorial day weekend I was up in the White Mountain, NH, ware these the first time. Wow I barely even feel them and they keep my legs warm enough yet breathe well. I did a quite tough hike but didn't feel the fatigue in my legs like I usually would feel .. Very pleased and had to come back here to let you know. :D ============= Surprising high quality product! Very happy with this purchase! Regard to the sizing: I'm a 5'7", 132 lbs, 30"w Asian (which means I have longer torso, shorter legs compare to most of you ugh lol). My regular size is 6. After reading many reviews here, I decided to ordered a LARGE, and am glad I did. It fit nice and smooth perfectly on my body without making me feel being squeezed ~ just the way I like (I'm one of those girls always wear lose comfy pants, not use to supper tight clothes). I think Medium would give more compression, but prefer this more comfy fit. These are 2 extra inches in length, I just fold them up around my ankles, not a problem at all.

Don't Hesitate - They're Worth It I'm a grad student with kids, so my budget is limited but I lead a very active lifestyle. I have a few pairs of random $5 - $7 cheap running leggings from Amazon, collected over the years. Some are better than others, but in general, they fall down or annoy me in one way or another. Had my first Tough Mudder obstacle course race coming up and wanted truly reliable running pants. These pants win on every level. They're the right height, never slip. Held up to a couple months of cross training, indoors as well as outdoors, and through the entire Mudder with all of its various obstacles. I didn't think about them once during the race, and to me, that is how you know you are wearing some good pants. Highly recommend. I got them on a lightning deal for $16, but would pay more. They're worth it. For sizing reference, I am 6'0" and 145 pounds and ordered a size small.

Best leggings EVER !! I absolutely love these leggings !! I’m 5’7” about 150lbs I wear A small I love the compression , I play outdoors with my son and we play with his water toys and these are great when they get wet they are not uncomfortable and dry super fast !! I live in these pants I think I already have like 10 pair 🤣 make sure you hang dry to retain shape if you do that they last a long time and are sooooooo worth every penny !

Quality and great price and great fit! After reading other reviews I decided to order one size up from my normal (XS) and got a Small. I am 5” and 112lbs lean build and they fit like a glove!! I highly recommend for someone looking for a true compression fit type leggings that will keep you warm and breathe at the same time

My Go To Scrubs Height: 5’2” Weight: 155 lbs Chest: 34DDD I personally love Adar brand scrubs because of the cool touch material. I have psoriasis and am sensitive to heat and heavier/scratchy fabrics. The cool touch material feels silky and smooth on my skin. It doesn’t retain heat and washes/dries well. My only wish was that the jogger style scrub pants and heathered colors Pro styles came in Petite. These are Medium size joggers and they’re just a little too long on me at 5’2”. They start out ok but stretch and get looser on a 16 hour shift. I feel like if they were made in Petite, they would fit like a glove. The waist band is very stretchy and has a lot of give. Easy to pull on and off when I’m rushing to get ready for work. These scrubs were fresh out of the package, fold marks and all. The only thing with the purple and wine colors are that they bleed a bit. So, it’ll either rub off on your skin or on your clothes if you wear a sweatshirt or jacket over your tops. You can usually tell by looking at the plastic poly bag, if there is color on the bag, then that same color will transfer to your skin and should be washed separate on its first wash. I carry most of my weight in my chest and my belly. I do not have big hips or a big booty. My curves are more so in my chest. The scrub top is just a smidgen tight on my midsection. I am a 34DDD and got a size Large top. It’s nothing that would deter me from wearing the top. If anything, just more incentive to take the stairs at work and hopefully drop a couple more lbs. Overall, happy customer 8/10

New favorite joggers I’m 5,3 and 105lbs with a mom bod. I got the XXS pants with XXS top. The pants are soft and light weight. I have skinny little chicken legs and these are the first pants that my legs aren’t swimming in. If you have thin legs especially around the calves these are for you. I still have room in the thighs. The butt was snug fitting which is weird cause I usually have no butt. Some stretch left in the butt though. Like no problem bending down. The waist band was a little bit snug but perfect for how I wanted it to fit. I usually wear a. XXS/ petite in FIG pants and have to tighten the waist. So it will be nice not to have to do that with these. The length was perfect too. Have not worn to work or washed so I can’t say how they will hold up. Overall I will buy more in the future. I might size up next time just because if we order tacos for lunch that waist band won’t have a lot of stretch left. Also you know how the dryer shrinks your clothing too Haha.

Toss the Figs, get Adar!!! I adore Adar! They’re reasonably priced and flattering to a curvier figure. I’m 5’6 & 183 pounds. I’m busty (38G) and I have wide hips (childbirth x 4). XL fits like a dream, doesn’t make me look frumpy, and lets me move freely enough to do yoga. (Yes, literally, pictures attached.) FINALLY, there’s a brand for me! Thank you, Adar! Cherokee is hit or miss with sizing that seems inherently catered more towards males. Figs only look good if the person wearing them is built like a stick figure. Carhartt has disappointed more than it’s ever delivered. But Adar is just stellar! This is my fourth pair, and I’m definitely gonna get them in every color. (Cough, cough…can a fan get a loyal customer discount?) Like I said, XL is perfect for me. I get the pants in XL Tall even though I’m only 5’6, just because I like to wear them higher (the waist band sits at my navel) and I like them to still cover my ankle/come down to the top of my shoes. Just a personal preference. I have these in black (3 sets) and teal (1 set). I’ve washed the black ones a gazillion times and they’ve never faded. I make my scrubs work as hard as I do, so I can say confidently that the seams and the material hold up to nearly-abusive wear. I fully anticipate being able to say the same about the teal set. Absolutely love these! Comfy, cute, and affordable. It’s a win! Seriously, give this company a try…they won’t let you down!

Odd proportions, odd sizing… just meh. I am 5’7”, 140lbs. I first bought the small because all my other scrubs are small. The small pants fit pretty good, maybe a little snug, but the calf was sooo tight. I’ve never had big calves so it’s an odd fit to be tight only there. So I returned and got the medium, which is what you see pictured. All seems good in the picture so I cut off the tags, thinking I had a keeper. BUT, after wearing them for a day, I feel like the fabric stretched out and the pants were slipping down, so I had to keep pulling them up. Usually I wear my scrub pants 2-3 days in a row if they’re not dirty looking but these were a 1 and done. Ok, I did wear them on day 2 just to see lol and I was losing my pants all day! Long story short- not a good fit unless you have scrawny stick legs. And prepare yourself for a baggier day 2.

misleading calling these “stretchy”.. “Stretchy” is a bit misleading with these pants. the overall fabric of these don’t really stretch at all. the waistband and ankle bands do, but that’s about it. I usually wear a medium in actually stretchy scrubs like the butter soft ones from uniform advantage. I got a large in these and I could not get them over my butt and I do not have a big butt at all but I have big thighs. I got an XL for a second go at these pants and they fit okay. I like them enough to keep them, but i wouldn’t buy another pair. the fabric is soft which is nice. I like the thick waistband. the calves are the tightest part, but I can squat and sit down without worrying about them ripping they just squeeze my calves while doing so. for reference I am 5’6” and 240lbs, I wear a size 16 in pants.

First impression My initial impression of these scrub bottoms are 9/10. The color is perfect, the fit is great & the style is appealing & functional. I consider these to be “faux joggers” because the bottoms are fitted but not like the actual jogger look, to me. I’m 5’5, 120 lbs & I got an XS-tall because I have long legs & I didn’t want the pants to be high waters & they fit perfectly, the waist band is comfortable & the strings help form the best fit! The only slight, is that the material feels like a dri-fit material & is therefore thin. I feel like this will be easy to clean up tho on those days you get a little bio-hazardous splatter. Overall I’m pleased. Hopefully they continue to look good after wash/wears

Satisfactory-Nothing Wonderful Fit-The fit was satisfactory. For reference, I'm 5'4" and ~180lbs. I need the extra large but it was unavailable. It's still wearable for exercise. Size up if you have a large stomach or thighs. Actually, I'm not convinced these are women's pants. These pants in the product photo seem to be on a man. What I mean is that the waist and hips follow one line, more of a male ratio in the photo. And they are long. Are these really for women? Quality-I don't think the fabric is that "technical", seems like the same pant you could find in some variation from the 80s until now. I like the striping, it doesn't advertise the brand. The big plus is that they are not leggings. Anything over leggings. I'm not going to add a bunch of qualifiers to sugarcoat the review. The branding, fit, and quality are off somehow. I think a big established company like "Addidas" can handle some skepticism. Satisfactory, but nothing wonderful.

Great quality, but the fit is a bit tricky… I was hoping to replace my old Adidas tapered athletic pants with these, but the fit is very different. They’re called tapered & they do narrow at the ankle, but there’s much looser at the crotch & thighs. [MY SIZE INFO:] I’m 5’8”, 145 lbs with fairly straight legs & not thick thighs. I’m long waisted & these are low rise, so I ordered them in Large to be sure they stayed up. Maybe I should have ordered a Medium, but the low rise is tricky for me so instead, I ended up with pants that are baggy in the thighs! Bottom line? The fit is fairly particular. If you know the design of these & your body type is right for them, they’re great. Nice, deep pockets + 12” long zippers at the ankle + a drawstring elastic waist. On me, they’re an overly comfy pair of athletic pants! **Thank you for reading this review & I hope that it’s !** 😊

Look like dress pants, feel like yoga wear These are comfortable, stretchy pants that have actual pockets. The pockets, combined with the pleats down the leg, make them look like dress pants, though they are made from a thick spandex material like yoga leggings. I find them comfortable (I refuse to wear pants I can't easily move and bend in), though I have to be careful what top I wear with them, due to my ample belly and apple-shaped body. For size reference, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 175 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in a large, and while they fit, they aren't flattering to my big belly (see my pics). They hit right at my ankle length-wise. Overall, I find these pants comfortable to wear, and know they will see a lot of use.

Comfortable pants that pass for business casual The material for these stretch pants is very similar to what you might expect for a yoga pant; soft, comfortable, true to size. I wish the waist band was a just a little thicker for an even smoother fit. They are easy to pull on and a good length if you’re a similar height as me - 5’3". I can wear both heels or with flats and they aren’t too short or drag the ground. The leg seams are a good touch to help pull them off as business casual/professional. They would be even better if they had a faux zipper/button, but they can pass for work despite the material feeling extremely casual. The pants will easily attract lint, dog hair, etc. but the debris easily bushes off. I recommend them if you’re looking to add a basic pant option to your work wardrobe.

Love the lightweight material and fit except the pockets I purchased these because I recently gained weight and needed something to cover all this extra fluff. These joggers were in line with my measurements and the material I wanted. We moved to Mexico last year, so I needed something lightweight and breathable. Since these are rayon spandex, I knew they would be perfect for the hot weather here. They fit as expected based on the size chart. I got the large. I'm 5'7", and in the photos I'm 175 lbs, 44 bust, 37 waist, 42.5 hips. My only complaint is the pockets. I know everyone wants pockets these days, but since rayon spandex is so lightweight, the tiny pockets in these joggers aren't deep enough to lay flat against the body. When I wear these, the pockets always end up puckering out in the front. If they were deeper and laid flat against the body, I wouldn't mind them so much.

I wish they offered the petite length in more colors I really like these pants. They’re comfortable, the fabric is flattering and doesn’t show off my cellulite and how out of shape I am. It’s thick and definitely not sheer. It’s stretchy, but hard to describe. I don’t think I have anything else like what these pants are made out of. I like that I can wear them with sneakers and a tee shirt, or put on a nice top to be a little more dressed up. I guess on me they kinda look a bit more like frumpy old lady pants, but honestly I don’t care. I’m 43 and I guess I am kinda frumpy, lol. The length on me is obviously not like it is in the listing photos. I’m 5’ 1” and have a longer torso and shorter legs, so everything is long on me. They’re still just barely cropped pants, and I think they look cute with my clogs. I’m not sure how much I’ll wear them in the winter, but we’ll see. I really love that they’ve got deep pockets. Like, I can fit my entire iPhone XS Max in there with room to spare. If only all women’s pants had pockets like these. I liked these so much I went ahead and ordered a different style of Ajisai joggers that aren’t offered in this shorter length. I’m hoping they’ll work out, but I suppose I can always have them tailored if they’re too long, but I really love them otherwise. I’d absolutely buy these in more colors if they offered them in the petite length. I’m just not interested in navy, although I may buy a second pair in black when they’re restocked. Finding cute pants when you’re short is so difficult. Especially when you don’t like to actually go out shopping and try things on. I’ve worn them several times now, and I’ve washed them and put them in the dryer on low, and they look perfect. They don’t collect lint and they don’t wrinkle easily. I’d definitely recommend them. Just be aware that if you’re short and not as skinny as you could be, (like me, I’m a size 10, creeping towards an 8) then you too are likely to look a bit frumpy in them rather than looking cool and statuesque like the model wearing them in the photos for the listing.

If you are petite size up on this one for loose comfortable fit I am 5'4" tall and 150 lbs. Most of my girth is in my waist, unfortunately. I wear a true Large in all pants. In this style, I originally bought the "Petite" Large. As you can see in the photos as marked, the large looks like a Medium on me, too tight and short? I also don't like how they hug my thighs as I expect joggers to feel loose and comfortable. The large felt close to wearing leggings on me. I ended up repurchasing an X-Large and it was much more comfortable and appears much better on me including the length. This is a very nice designed pant, the fabric is like high end sportswear. This pant would be great for traveling not only to wear on the plane but fit well and light in carry on luggage. I hope my review helps someone else!

Small fits awkwardly but willing to give another chance These aren't terrible and I really wanted to like them, but the small fits awkwardly. I get that these are supposed to be workout/travel pants, but I'm looking for something that would be presentable in a casual office setting while still comfortable for keeping up with small children. These aren't crazy huge but they're too loose in the waist/thighs. The waist can obviously be pulled tighter, but then the material just bulges out and looks frumpy. I'm 120 lbs and typically a size small/6/27-28" waist so I was surprised by how big these were. On the plus side, the quality of the material is nice and they're very comfortable so I can see why there are so many positive reviews. I'm 5'2 with average proportions, so the regular length was fine. However, I think I do prefer the slightly cropped look that these are intended to have, so I'll try to exchange these for a petite x-small if they come back in stock and will update my review accordingly!

I’m obsessed with these pants! You can sneaky sneaky wear them for everything! In the last week I’ve worn these on a run, on a plane, out on a power walk, and out to a fancy dinner. Love these pants These pants are perfect for fitness or any casual event. They fit well, are soft, stretchy and silky. May be my new favorite pants. Another reviewer commented they are good for a curvy figure and I agree with this. I am on the petite side, but proportionally curvy and have a time trying to find pants that are both comfortable and flattering. These pants fit the bill. I originally got the petite size M because other reviewers my height (5'3") were happy with the petite length. But they looked goofy on me - too short. Perhaps my legs are proportionately longer than my torso for a short person. I would advise you to actually measure your inseam if you're deciding which length to order. Else, I ordered according to the size chart and found it to be accurate. You can see in my photo that I also tried the cloud color which was beautiful, but the seams of the pockets showed through the light colored fabric. I am very happy with the black pants, though, and would consider getting another pair but stick to the dark colors to avoid the pocket seams showing.

I ordered these sweatpants thinking I was going to receive soft, warm, wide-legged pants. Instead these are a weird slick material that is cold to the touch and not a texture I like on my skin. I'm 5'5, 135 lbs and I ordered the medium and the pants fit me well, they don't drag the floor and the waistband sits comfortably at my waist. It has nice deep pockets, and the pants look nice from a distance. However, up close they are a slightly shiny slick material that reminds me of gym shorts, not the type of material that is prone to warmth. Overall, although the pants fit me well and seem well made, I will not be wearing them. If you decide to purchase these be aware the material is not typical sweatpants material!

I think these khakis are great! They have gotten me through all the seasons, washed, and dried. The color is typically khaki color, cotton, so definitely consider drying. I knew buying the 4 would be a little big but anticipated shrinking them some and now they fit perfect, not as small as a 2 though. They were long on me and I could hem them but just decided to fold them under and they stay very nicely. I wear here with booties, sandals, and keds. Love these joggers! I am 5'2" and weigh 115 lbs. My inseam is 29" and my waist is about the same. I ordered a small because I wanted these pants to be loose for travel, and they did not disappoint! For a more fitted, polished look, the XS would have probably fit as well. They were plenty long enough and did not run up my ankles. We went on a 3 week trip to Europe and they felt like Pajamas on the long plane ride. They have washed and dried very well. No pilling so far. The rise is perfect for me. The waistband is very comfortable. The drawstring made me chuckle because it is very long, but that is no big deal. The material is very soft but keeps its shape. I wore these pants several days on vacation before washing then, and they held up great. The pockets are big enough to fit my Samsung S22 Ultra securely! They also dry quickly. Great buy for the money.

These are just awful, too small in the waist, too big in the leg and huge in the ankle I bought the Terry relaxed fit jogger version of these and I love them! I'm 5'7, weigh 225-230 and wear an 18 in jeans. I bought the Terry ones in an xl and they are a perfect fit. They sit at my waist, they don't pinch and give me that funky muffin top, the legs are form fitting without being suffocatingly tight and they taper down and hug my ankles. They were out of colors I wanted so I figured these would work, ya know same brand same style. I have no idea how they went soooo wrong with these but they are just awful!!! They are tight in the waist giving me muffin top, the fupa is on full display and yet the legs are huge! I could fit both of my ankles into one ankle hole and still had room to move around. The closest I could describe these to are men's joggers from Walmart. I hate them. I wish I hadn't bought 3 pairs and instead just waited for the colors I wanted to be restocked in the Terry style. 😮‍💨

If I were writing a review on just the Dark Grey pants, it would be 5 stars. The pants are a light, soft, comfortable material. Product says “order true to size”. Reading through all the reviews, some say order a size up, others say order a size down. It was a dilemma but in the end I decided on a Medium and they fit me wonderfully. I loved them so much that the next day I ordered the same pants in Navy. They are an inch smaller on the waist and length. I am hesitant to order any other colour because I do not know if the size will be like the Dark Grey or like the Navy. In fact, I do not know if the sizing is consistent even in the same colour. For the record, my waist is 34” and I am 5'0" but, sorry, this won’t help you because the cut is so different. UPDATE JUNE 15, 2021 - A large in the navy was finally in stock and I ordered it. It is WAY too big, at least 4 inches bigger in the waist than the grey Medium. So if a Medium navy was too small and a large too big, I don't know where that leaves me. Lately I've been doing nothing but exchanging and returning items. I have officially given up ordering clothes off Amazon.

Slightly disappointed after reading reviews that the length were good for taller women, not in my case. I'm 5'7 and they're a good inch or two too short but got them for work. Bought 2 pairs so bit miffed about length but can't fault anything else. Super comfortable and great material

Good fit, low price, but no pockets I'm a plus size uni student who just wanted to find some comfy pants to be lazy in. I have plenty of lounge pants but I keep seeing people wear plaid pj bottoms to class, so I decided I want to follow the trend. I'm 5'5, usually size 18/20, and short waisted, and these pants fit me great. The rise hits right at/below my belly button, and the pants legs are a great length - a little long w/o shoes (but not so long that I trip) but with shoes I think they'll hit right at the heel. As far as material and construction goes, I am disappointed there are no pockets, and the material isn't a true flannel; it's very lightweight, almost on the flimsy side, and doesn't seem like it would hold up to a lot of walking, especially with my thunder thighs. Additionally, the seams are a little rough, but I think after washing they'll soften. All in all, not the best pj pants you could buy, but for $14 on a college student budget, they work.

Nice pajamas that run big These are very nice, soft, pajama bottoms. They have a flannel feel but are not thick. As other reviewers have mentioned, they run big. I ordered a size Small and was worried they might be snug but they are loose on me, which is perfect for wearing in bed. The length is perfect for me too. I am 5' 7" 135lbs and typically wear a Small or Medium. I would have been swimming in the Medium.

Red and “White” polka dots no more They looked cute, cherry red with White dots. But the fabric smelled awful, so I washed them in cold water before wearing. The dots are no longer white. They are all pink, and not so cute any more. Thank goodness for the Color Catcher I tossed into the machine. It “caught” much of the red dye, but not enough. To address other reviews: yes, this is flannel, which is a napped cotton that can be thick or lightweight. Both are still “flannel”. And yes, they are long, which works for me at 5’8”.
comfy I’m only around 5 feet tall, so I expected them to be a few inches too long. However, with some easy alterations, they fit perfectly! They’re not flannel, but super comfortable nonetheless.

Loose, comfy, thin - Size M measurements below Measurements of size M/Medium in inches: -Full Length: 41” -Inseam: 29” -Width/waistband: 15” -Front rise: 12” -Ankle: 5” (measured after washing/drying) It’s soft, stretchy, lightweight, breathable, and flexible. I fell in love with it instantly. I bought it in every color over the months when I saw a sale. Keep in mind it’s thin and can be damaged if washed/dried improperly. It can be see-through if you bend over or if it’s form fitting on you. Imagine a panty hose. The more it stretches the more visible the skin is underneath. It’s loose from my butt to legs so I don’t have to worry. Since I have skinny ankles and calves, the cuffs rise up during sleep. It pills a little bit around the ankles. I usually prefer a jogger with a drawstring but for sleeping no. It’s hard for me to find comfortable sleeping pants. I guess I’m picky. It’s even harder to find one with pockets. This hits almost all of my checkboxes for a sleeping pant. I am only 4’11” and 95 lbs, but I ordered a medium since I prefer to wear all of my pants low-rise. I have a pudgy sensitive stomach. High-waist is not comfortable to me. I am one of the few waiting for that trend to end. Well, it doesn’t have to end but why not design low-rise at the same time? I don’t really have any complaints besides a suggestion: Please make a silky satin version! -Made in Philippines -95% modal, 5% elastane -Machine wash cold -Gentle cycle -Tumble dry low ——————- *UPDATE/EDIT* I lost weight over the year so I repurchased all four colors in size Small. 1 out of 4 is bigger than usual. Did they sow the wrong size tag? It feels more like a medium. I encountered the same problem with their thicker joggers. Inconsistent sizing is annoying because it is a hassle to return. However, I will put up with it because I love these joggers! I’m also sad they discontinued the light blue colors.

Exactly what I wanted! I wanted a lightweight, soft pair of lounge pants for the house. These are perfect! They are super-soft, light and cool, and the cuffs fit perfectly without being tight. One of my best purchases from Amazon! I'm 5'7", 140 lbs and the medium is a perfect fit.

Larger than Expected. Nice Material for House/Sleep Pants These night pants run a little big and baggy. I have a waist size of 27 inches so I thought the S would be good, but the waist band fell to my hip bone. As far as the length, I’m 5’4" with short legs, and I thought it was a little too long for me. The length from the waist to the bottom is 37 inches and the inseam is 26 inches for size S. I think the XS would have fit me better. I've included pictures of the measurements for size S. Otherwise, I really like the material of the pants. It is very soft, it as some stretch to it and a little sheer. These are good to wear around the house, sleep in, and they will keep you cool on a warm Texas day; I wouldn’t wear them outside. The pants also have pockets which is a nice addition and makes them very convenient for stashing things when moving about the house.

Very comfortable but strictly at-home pants for me These are, as described, a nice, lightweight pair of sweats (I don't really think they look like joggers because of the thin elastic at the bottom, but maybe that's just me). They fit a bit on the large side, especially through the hips and bum on me. The tie waist is adjustable, so they stay up, but they're loose everywhere else. The material is very soft. I washed on cold and dried on medium (hoping they might shrink a bit); they didn't shrink or fade. These are very comfortable at-home pants, but I wouldn't wear them out because of the fit and the fact that they're so thin. However, for lounging around, they're awesome. I couldn't wear them to jog because they're too loose and they would chafe. I'l have a 34" inseam and the length is just right. I would worry that they'd be too short if my legs were longer, and too long if I was very petite; I would say 5'4"-5'11" would be about the right range is this size (just guessing, though). If I were to order another pair, I'd probably size down to a medium because I prefer them a bit more fitted in the booty. For reference, I'm 5'10", 180 lbs, 36dd, 32" waist, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the large.

Very comfortable but strictly at-home pants for me These are, as described, a nice, lightweight pair of sweats (I don't really think they look like joggers because of the thin elastic at the bottom, but maybe that's just me). They fit a bit on the large side, especially through the hips and bum on me. The tie waist is adjustable, so they stay up, but they're loose everywhere else. The material is very soft. I washed on cold and dried on medium (hoping they might shrink a bit); they didn't shrink or fade. These are very comfortable at-home pants, but I wouldn't wear them out because of the fit and the fact that they're so thin. However, for lounging around, they're awesome. I couldn't wear them to jog because they're too loose and they would chafe. I'l have a 34" inseam and the length is just right. I would worry that they'd be too short if my legs were longer, and too long if I was very petite; I would say 5'4"-5'11" would be about the right range is this size (just guessing, though). If I were to order another pair, I'd probably size down to a medium because I prefer them a bit more fitted in the booty. For reference, I'm 5'10", 180 lbs, 36dd, 32" waist, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the large.

Lightweight, heat-proof basic comfort These sweatpants are quite comfortable, especially in hot weather, though very basic and cheap. A medium fits me just fine (5’7” woman, size 8-10), length is right, and the fit is perfectly loose all through the legs. While the fabric reminds me of a cheap airplane blanket, I appreciate how lightweight the pants are, particularly in warm/hot weather. It really feels like wearing almost nothing. Even though I’ve snagged the pants on some sharp objects, so far the material hasn’t ripped. The white drawstring is a nice touch too. It’s just a shame that these pants don’t have pockets. I’d definitely feel like these pants were more worth the price if they came with pockets.

Black women's joggers Small women's drawstring joggers. These are very nice soft and good quality. They do run larger in size the drawstring helps keep them up. I ordered a small and they are more like a size medium. I am 5'4 and 110lbs and these are large on me. They are long with a slight gather at the ankle that isn't tight. The joggers are comfortable and great loung pants. The color is also great they are black with grey in them. Great joggers just remember to order a size down.

Forgiving sizing This size small could easily also fit a medium. I am 5 ft. 5 inches tall, 127 lbs, and I would still fit these if I gained a few pounds, TONS of stretch in the waistband.

Pretty great - But doesn’t match photo I’m 5’3” and 150ish lbs. I got the large and it fits well. I didn’t think I’d ever be able to find joggers that actually hit me above the ankle. I’m short to begin with and then I also have a longer torso so my legs are extra short. I’m pretty annoyed that the string is gray. I was specifically looking for a pair with a white string (like how the photo advertises itself to be). I’m keeping because they do fit well but I’m still pretty annoyed about it.

Comfy! These are so cute! I’m only 5’1”. Finding joggers that aren’t super long is nearly impossible. These are perfect though and so comfy. I’m 125lbs and 6 months post partum. These are perfectly comfy and cute for lazy days when I still want a bit of style. I’d definitely buy again!

Petite ladies: stock up **Update** Two months later these are about 1"-2" shorter in length. But I love them so much I have ordered a second pair. --- The perfect at home pants. I am 5' even and 130lbs. I ordered a small and the fit is like it was custom made for me. I don't wear them as capris though they are the perfect length to be sweatpant joggers. The waist is midrise and it is fitted enough to not feel like a slob and loose enough to not be immodest. Love them!

Comically large on me! (Update, I bought 2 sizes down and they fit! - so soft!) I purchased and just received a pair of these in light blue, in an extra-small, and they fit just right! And just like the grey ones, they are amazingly soft. The length isn’t as short as on the model, but since my legs are relatively longer than my body, they do come up above my ankles. I just love love love the super softness of them. These fit to my body better. Maybe I’ll get even another pair! ———- From 2 to 3 to 4 stars - sooooooooo soft. Just note they run really big. 2.0 stars for that. 5 stars for how soft they are! Updated review: even though I laughed at these and they are still ridiculously big on me, I find that I reach for them to wear around the house more and more! When I was sick for a couple days recently, these felt super soft and comfortable. And even just wearing them around when it’s cold outside and I’m not going anywhere. I’m going to see what other sizes they have- a small would be a little better but an x-small would be even better. Still, I seem to be liking these SUPER soft slouchy looking pants! I had to increase my stars to 4 though since they are still, after many washings, the softest sweatpants I have ever owned. Ugly on, still though, as they are too big. Leaving original review below. —— I actually laughed when I took these out of the package they were shipped in. They are ridiculously big for size mediums. I expected a loose fit as I ordered a medium to have the loose fit (even though I wear a small), but these are more like a loose relaxed-fit large. They are like the old sweatpants people used to wear, except the waistband comes down lower, which I like, along with the little tie, which updates them some—however they may be riding down lower simply because they are so danged big at the waist. Mine do not come up higher on the leg as the model shows, but I’m only 5’2”; however they don’t pool or bunch at the ankle. They aren’t cute on because they are so big but I question if they’d be cute on even had they fit better. With that waist that is way to big for me, and the hips super wide while the legs are fitted at the ankle, this gives these a comical look on me! They do have two pockets. They’re soft inside. They are fairly thick so they’d be warm. I washed and dried them and they came out so super soft! I wish they fit better because of that incredible softness. I’d only wear these around the house to clean house or if I were feeling bad and wanted something big and loose and soft and comfortable, which these are. I can’t see the price to know that comparison to quality, but these ‘sweatpants’ do look and feel well-made, with no hanging threads or wonky stitching. If these had been sized better, I’d give them at least 4 stars, for the softness and comfy aspect. Better size down! I hope my review helped you today.

An odd length that looks and feels funny These are made of a really nice fleece-lined fabric that is soft and warm without being too thick or bulky. I love that they have deep pockets and an adjustable drawstring waist and are baggy and stretchy. What I don't love: I know these are advertised as "cropped". However, they aren't cropped at a length that works well for my 5'5" height. If you are 5'7" and up, these are cropped and hit you about like they do on the Amazon model. If you are 5'5" or 5'4", they look and feel like pants you put in the dryer and they shrunk and now you look dorky and embarrassing. They aren't cropped enough to be anything but just shy of ankle length and this feels weird and uncomfortable when you move or walk or sleep. Basically, they are too short to be pants but not short enough to register as capris. The length annoys my ankles and I'm constantly pulling them down or up, ANYTHING but the weird spot where they choose to hug my legs. If you are 5'3" and under, these will fit just like regular joggers, no "cropped" style at all. Also, while I like these a bit baggy, I ordered a small and they fit a tad big on my 26" waist, the waist sagging down a lot if I don't tie them. I prefer pants that are low-rise and sit below my navel for comfort but if you are tiny/thin, realize that you will be pulling these up alot.

Soft, comfy and warm These are really nice joggers. I have several pairs but none are fleece-lined, so these are my only winter-appropriate pair. They're lightweight but warm. I have some similar pants that are meant for winter wear but they're very heavy and make me itch (I have eczema). These are very soft, not bulky, stretchy, and seem well-made (stitching and material are all solid). They washed well in cold and I hung them to dry. We weren't given a size choice on these, and I ended up with a medium. If I wanted the fit in the picture (the looser, somewhat slouchy look), I would need the large for my size (XL would be too big in the waist). However, the mediums are very comfortable even though they're more fitted, and IME these types of pants stretch out after awhile. I'm very happy with these and will get a lot of use out of them since I work from home most days (and have 2 dogs). For reference, I'm 5'10", 180 lbs, 36dd, 32" waist, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the medium.

Great joggers These joggers are so comfy! They are soft sweatpant material on the outside and fleecy on the inside. They are a lighter weight. The length is perfect for me, I always have issues with the length of joggers. They go right above my ankles so I can scrunch them up to mid calf and they don't look too bunchy. The leg cuffs stay in place. They are a medium rise, and a slimmer style in the legs, not too tight like leggings, but not sloppy and baggy. I think they run pretty true to size, maybe a bit bigger, but sweatpants should be comfy, so I wouldn't size down unless you are between sizes. I'm 5'9, 165lbs, and I ordered my normal size, medium. They have a drawstring waist and pockets. The quality seems very good, I think these will last a while. I'm definitely going to stock up on these!

I've searched high and low for a capri/cropped jogger and finally found it. They are not the thin, flimsy or shiny material, they are a nice soft and for me whose always cold they are warm and the drawstring actually works. For reference I'm 5'4, around 150lbs,and wear anywhere between a small medium or large lol I also have chronic inflammation so I went with the large to be on the safe side. I find the large does run a little big even when tied and they will stretch as well. I would say order your regular size.
