Really soft on the inside. I’m short and these are definitely still a cropped fit (I’m okay with this) some reviews mentioned them fitting like normal joggers but unless you’re under 5 feet I don’t see how this would be the case. I typically am between a size 8&10. I got the medium and still have a bit of room in my thigh, and had to tighten the waist drawstrings a bit. I maaaay have been able to get away with a small but I think it would have been snug.

Good bang for your buck I only give 4 stars because I did not find the sizing to be accurate. I got these for a work trip, the husband loves them and he’s not normally a pants-complimenter. I really like the length but they did run tight on my thighs so there was some pull in the crotch area. I had looked at previous reviews and thought I would be a size 2 and I liked the short look so I got them in short (I think!). I think the quality is good for the cost. Unfortunately I did not have time to make a return before the work trip and now they are my back up work pants. I have a small waist so I always have a hard time finding proportional pants that fit my thighs, butt, and waist but I am overall fairly skinny. 125 lbs and 5’5. I would buy again and in different colors if I needed more work clothes since they are so affordable but I WFH.

Skinny pants that fit short women I tried on the navy size 2 short as well as a size 0. Let me tell you, for being very short at 5’0, normally “short” sizes still have an extra 2 inches more of length past my ankles that i have to roll up, and “cropped” sizes fit me like normal pants. These pants on the other hand fit me perfectly length-wise at the ankle, so they might fit a little high if you have normal-sized legs. The waist sits high and fits a little loose while the crotch bunches up a bit, but wearing a belt helps that problem. I normally fit between 0-2 (mostly 2’s), and these pants are a skinny-fit, so I recommend you try these on in your true or bigger size range.

Thick, stretch, nice quality black pants! I am 5’5” about 120-125lbs. These pants fit perfectly!! I was looking for pants this length. So if shorter or taller, you might want to adjust which type of pants you buy. They are stretchy so they fit nicely without looking like leggings. The material is thick so they look and feel like nice quality. My only concern is the waist. I’m concerned if the fasteners will cause the pants to stick out in the front? I will update this post if after I wear them a few times this happens. But for now, I am super excited have a new pair of travel pants!!

Very skewed sizing. Ordered a 2 and the tag said 2/30. Ordered a 0 and the tag said 0/28. I was looking for a staple work pant. I normally wear a size 2 or 26. I ordered a 2 and they were way too large. I ordered a 0 and they were still way too large. Since they’re an amazon brand pant I had high expectations. Well I looked at the size tags and the 2 says 30 and the 0 says 28. That is incredibly skewed sizing. Most people that wear a size 30 would be in more of a 6, 8, 10 depending on the brand. I’m very disappointed and will be returning both pairs. I was looking forward to pursuing other Amazon Essentials items but now I have to reconsider. NOTE: I did refer to the size chart prior to ordering and it does say 0=25 inch waist and 2=26 inch waist.

Perfect fit The size 14 fits appropriately. Im a size 14, 165lbs and 5'5" and these fit like a glove. They are ankle pants so I've been wearing them with cute boots during the winter. Come summer, these will be cute with flats or sandals. The front pockets are fake but the butt pockets are real. The material is a bit stiff but I think with more wear they'll get softer. Overall, I like them!

Yellow is mustard, fit might run small for some Personally I ordered the yellow because it’s mustard!!!! I can see where some might say it runs small as there’s not much difference between sizes…. Im about a size 12 in most skinny jeans/jeggings (my waist is about 33-34” & hips 43” (I have kind of a flat butt)) & I think I’ll keep both the 12 & 14 unless the 12 shrinks in the wash a ton! 12 fits a bit tighter (but not uncomfortably so) & 14 more roomy comfort. Even tho I’m only 5’3, i’m short waisted (I don’t find the rise too high like mom jeans), & my legs are longer (33” inseam) & these are the perfect length on me! Folded inseam 25”, unfolded 27”. Fit is more of a slim straight leg pant which works well for my more athletic build legs/hips. Curvier gals may want to size up another size to accommodate any hips or butt? Fits like a sausage casing! The fabric and construction are very nice on these pants. Made in Bangladesh from 95% Cotton, 5% Elastane, the fabric is a decent thickness, the buttons are well sewn and it even has a full metal YKK zipper. The fit is where these lost stars for me. They are super tight in the waist on me. Really they are tight all over but they are most uncomfortable in the waist. The length is ok on me, in fact if I uncuff them I could even wear them as a full length pant if they were not so tight! For size reference I am 5'4" 150lbs, pear shaped and typically wear a size 8 or 10 in pants. In the picture I am squeezed into the size 8 in black. I don’t even think the size 10 would work. These pants are also very soft and have a brushed feel to them, in black they attract lint like crazy, so consider a lighter color or keep a lint roller handy at all times!

My New Favorite Lounge Pants Addendum: Starting to unravel at waistband after five months of regular use. Length - I'm 5' 7", normal weight, and the length was perfect with the waistband up at the waist. Waist - I have a 30" waist and a large was a bit big. I had to secure it with the string. Maybe some people wear it lower on the hips but I like it at my waist. Color - Perfect grey blue. Dressing is easier if everything is some shade of blue. Material - Too warm for outside in warm weather but I wear them in the mornings when the AC is on and cold. The material is heavier than my lightest sweatpants but way lighter than my heaviest. Wash - I've washed them four times with no shrinkage. Comfort - Your not even aware of them other than the fact your legs aren't cold. Price & Quality - Cheaper and higher quality than I could have gotten from Walmart. Addendum: So comfortable I put them on every time they come out of the wash while at home. Not quite stylish enough to wear out and about.

SHRINKS 2 INCHES IN LENGTH!!! Purchased an XL based on size chart. I am 5'4" with a 30" inseam. Great, thick, cozy fabric. Nice deep pockets. I tried them on and was very pleased with the fit. I have chemical allergies, so I washed [cold] and dried [low heat] so I could wear these without risking a rash. I would love to love these but NOPE.UNACCEPTABLE: A full 2" shrinkage in the length of the inseam [from 31" down to 29"] and the out seam [from 41" down to 38.5"]: see the before and after photos. The back crotch length is also now too short [ye old wedgie and dip waist]. The width shrinkage was apparent [about an inch] but not too snug. As all the regular sizes have stated 30.5" inseams, I assumed that they would stay within 1/2" of that measurement.From the comments I read the quality control of these sweatpants seems poor. A 10% loss in length is not OK.So lost 3 stars on comfort and style for the wedgie and too short legs. SO, AMAZON, if you want to fix this and use the same fabric, you would need to add 2" to the length and 1" to the circumference to allow for shrinkage.

am 5'4/130lbs/belly bulge.. Got size 'x-small regular' and they fit me nicely, not tight but, snug enough. to me these leggings run on large side for xsmall should of been skin tight like yoga pants on me.. Not my favorite I ordered a 3x based on reviews. They fit too large, but not sure a 2x would have been too tight. Wanted to give myself a little room with as many reviews saying they ripped. Not my favorite I ordered a 3x based on reviews. They fit too large, but not sure a 2x would have been too tight. Wanted to give myself a little room with as many reviews saying they ripped. I have a big badonk and huge thighs. I wear a US size 20-ish. 5'5" The legs are weird. I was hoping for wide leg, but these are just cut wide everywhere and slightly less wide at the ankle, if that makes sense. They feel good on. A tad stiff, but ok. Smooth. Thick. They just are not flattering at all. Honestly spent a day hating my body after trying them on. Only kept them because I needed pants that weren't skinny jeans. And the color is gorgeous. Hit my feet, but I'm sure they will shrink up a bit in the wash. This fabric relaxes A LOT during wear, so be aware that what might fit at the beginning of the day may not by the end of the day.

Nice enough to wear out of the house! Edit- April 21, 2020;I’ve had these for almost a year now and I’m baffled by those who say these have dramatic shrinkage. I wash these in cold water and air dry when I can, but my husband often does laundry and he tosses them in the dryer on high heat with everything else. Yet these still fit me despite the fact that I’ve gained 20 pounds (corticosteroids suck). They still barely graze the ground, and are still baggy in the legs. However they are more snug around my stomach and butt than I’d prefer, but that’s due to me growing, not the pants shrinking.Original review;I ordered a medium, because I’m 5’4 and my ever fluctuating weight is currently 140. You can see by the pictures that they are fairly baggy on me. The length just grazed the ground when I wore sneakers. From the reviews, I knew that there was a chance that medium could be too big, but knowing that baggy is more forgiving than tight, I took the chance. But I was conservative with my purchase and just ordered one pair so I could gauge the size. (I want ALL of the cute prints!)I’ve been looking for cute print poplin pants for a long time, and I was overjoyed when I discovered that Amazon carried them at such an affordable price! The Amazon Essentials Poplin Pajama Pants are the cutest and most affordable ones on Amazon! They are very reminiscent (style wise, they are close to identical!) to my favorite Old Navy poplin pajama pants. These are my new favorites. The prints are gorgeous! I can’t wait to buy more! The only way they could be more perfect is for them to have pockets. But that’s not enough of a deficit for me to deduct a star, as I seriously adore these!I discovered a long time ago that poplin pants are just as cool and cute as linen pants, but usually much more affordable. The trick is to find cute prints as opposed to stripes or plaids which will always look like pajamas no matter what you wear them with.These pants are perfect for summer! Especially on days when you will be transitioning a lot from outdoors to inside where it’s cool and air conditioned, or knowing you will be out until evening, but won’t have a chance to change into something warmer. The fabric is smooth and cool so you don’t get too hot, but covers your legs completely so you don’t get too cool. I packed mine to take to a family camp in mid June in Oregon when the weather ranges from low 60s to high 80s. They were perfect! I couldn’t have been more comfortable! They even held up beautifully when my husband and I went on a hike and climbed down a cliff to get to a creek.And one last thing I have to get off my chest... It kills me when I read the reviews complaining that these are “not a soft cotton”. Obviously these people didn’t have any idea what poplin was before they ordered them. Lack of knowledge on the customer’s part is not a defect of the product and leaving a negative review as a result shows that they are irresponsible consumers.If you’re looking for poplin pants, look no further! Buy these, you won’t regret it.

I need a thousand more pairs *** For size reference I am 5'3", 180# and purchased size XL. L would have been fine. They do run long. Notice in photo rolled up legs. I must say they've quickly become a firm favorite. Here's why: Utmost Comfort: The moment I slipped into these pants, they became a fave. The fabric feels soft against the skin, making bedtime even more inviting. Fit: These pants are roomy. They don't constrict, yet they don’t sag, ensuring maximum comfort throughout the night. Breathable Material: The material is lightweight and breathable. No more waking up feeling too warm; these pants ensure optimal temperature regulation. Stylish Design: While they’re meant for sleeping, their chic design and fit mean I feel put together even during those impromptu video calls with friends just before bed. The cactus print is sooo cute. Durable Through Washes: After several laundry cycles, the color remains vibrant, the fabric retains its texture, and there's no discernible shrinkage. In a market flooded with sleepwear options, the Amazon Essentials Poplin Sleep Pant genuinely stands out. For those seeking a blend of style, comfort, and durability in their nightwear, this is a must-have. Sweet dreams guaranteed!

Pink dots , not white. Lightweight. The elastic is not tight which is fantastic. Usually the elastic is way too tight. Do not dry. They shrink. I have had multiple pairs. Have MULTIPLE pair👍⭐️ I own three pair of these PJ bottoms. I’m very picky with the fabric for any PJ bottoms. Poplin is the best for me. I rub my feet and legs when I sleep and need a durable material. These don’t leave little fabric pills all over my sheet. They are comfortable to wear. The waist is a draw cord. I call these my house-wearing pants because when I’m home, all I wear are PJ bottoms. I WISH THEY HAD POCKETS. That is the only issue. They are always a little longer and bigger, but once I wash them, they fit perfectly—the NAVY, FLORAL pair I’ve had since May and washed numerous times. As you can see, they still fit perfectly. I ordered a LARGE, and they fit me as a LARGE usually does. (5’ 7”, 145 - 150 lbs) MATERIAL: 97% Cotton 3% Elastane WASHING: I washed them in WARM water 💦 on the REGULAR cycle and tumbled dried on NORMAL. The PRICE for these PJ Bottoms is in the usual range. They’re exactly as advertised and well-made.

Perfect, as pj bottoms! (but not for outside wear) Pretty perfect as PJs: 97% cotton, 3% elastitcine (except a label inside has the caution "Keep Away from Fire"). Truth is, I ordered them to wear outside for exercise outside on hot days, because the photos on Amazon looked like they could pass for pants. But they are more baggy - especially in the hips -- than depicted in the product description online, and have the pj string tie in front. In other words, they are classic, perfect pajama bottoms. They are cool, crisp and comfortable. But I already have 2 pair of summer cotton pjs from Amazon that I love, so I don't need more pjs. I wouldn't feel good wearing these PJs outside, so I'll probably return them. Size info: I'm 5-5", 122 lbs, and wearing Small in the photos (socks only). I gave them 5 stars as pajamas, but I will probably return them. Will someone please design flattering lightweight cotton pants for us walkers and exercisers to wear outside on hot days? !)

Fabric is fading, I hope they last I guess I'm a skinflint but I can't see paying $30-$40 for something nobody else every sees. I've been buying Old Navy pj pants for years because they were cute, the price was right and they fit me. Old Navy's quality has gone downhill recently, their fabric rips and frays, the stitching comes apart and I won't even pay their sale prices anymore. I decided to try Amazon since they had few cute patterns available in my size and the price is similar to Old Navy. The fit of the Amazon pj pants is very similar to the way the Old Navy pants fit, I'm 5'3" 140 lbs and the mediums aren't too tight and just slightly too long. I like the drawstring on the Amazon pj pants, it's kind of a substantial corded ropey-tape thing like Old Navy used to have years ago. I've already washed the Amazon pjs 3 times and I notice that they're fading a little but otherwise the fabric and stitching is holding up. I'll try and remember to write an update in a few months.

40” waist without being stretched is not an XL. That is way too big for an XL. An XL should be 36” in the waist without being stretched, and up to 38” when being stretched, according to their size chart (I took a screenshot of the size chart for these). As you can see in the 2nd photo, the waist is 40” (20” x 2) without being stretched in the slightest. It won’t stay up because it’s so loose. I think the pair they sent me should have been XXL instead of XL. I can’t wear these, and because I waited until the weather warmed up again briefly before I washed and wore them for the first time, now I believe I am 2 days late to request a replacement. Uugh!

The style is classic and casual, perfect for the wearing inside the house. Size wise, I am 5'3" and 135lb, my usual size is S. I did chose the S when ordering, after trying it, I think it is a little bit large for me. I am not going to return or exchange, I personally don't have issue wearing slight larger pants, especially sleep pants. If you want the exact fit, I would recommend ordering one size smaller.

Good bang for your buck I only give 4 stars because I did not find the sizing to be accurate. I got these for a work trip, the husband loves them and he’s not normally a pants-complimenter. I really like the length but they did run tight on my thighs so there was some pull in the crotch area. I had looked at previous reviews and thought I would be a size 2 and I liked the short look so I got them in short (I think!). I think the quality is good for the cost. Unfortunately I did not have time to make a return before the work trip and now they are my back up work pants. I have a small waist so I always have a hard time finding proportional pants that fit my thighs, butt, and waist but I am overall fairly skinny. 125 lbs and 5’5. I would buy again and in different colors if I needed more work clothes since they are so affordable but I WFH.

Skinny pants that fit short women I tried on the navy size 2 short as well as a size 0. Let me tell you, for being very short at 5’0, normally “short” sizes still have an extra 2 inches more of length past my ankles that i have to roll up, and “cropped” sizes fit me like normal pants. These pants on the other hand fit me perfectly length-wise at the ankle, so they might fit a little high if you have normal-sized legs. The waist sits high and fits a little loose while the crotch bunches up a bit, but wearing a belt helps that problem. I normally fit between 0-2 (mostly 2’s), and these pants are a skinny-fit, so I recommend you try these on in your true or bigger size range.

Thick, stretch, nice quality black pants! I am 5’5” about 120-125lbs. These pants fit perfectly!! I was looking for pants this length. So if shorter or taller, you might want to adjust which type of pants you buy. They are stretchy so they fit nicely without looking like leggings. The material is thick so they look and feel like nice quality. My only concern is the waist. I’m concerned if the fasteners will cause the pants to stick out in the front? I will update this post if after I wear them a few times this happens. But for now, I am super excited have a new pair of travel pants!!

Very skewed sizing. Ordered a 2 and the tag said 2/30. Ordered a 0 and the tag said 0/28. I was looking for a staple work pant. I normally wear a size 2 or 26. I ordered a 2 and they were way too large. I ordered a 0 and they were still way too large. Since they’re an amazon brand pant I had high expectations. Well I looked at the size tags and the 2 says 30 and the 0 says 28. That is incredibly skewed sizing. Most people that wear a size 30 would be in more of a 6, 8, 10 depending on the brand. I’m very disappointed and will be returning both pairs. I was looking forward to pursuing other Amazon Essentials items but now I have to reconsider. NOTE: I did refer to the size chart prior to ordering and it does say 0=25 inch waist and 2=26 inch waist.

Perfect fit The size 14 fits appropriately. Im a size 14, 165lbs and 5'5" and these fit like a glove. They are ankle pants so I've been wearing them with cute boots during the winter. I’m 4’11” weight about 135lb. I ordered a 12 short. They are a little big in the waist and hips. They fit like high waisted pants. The inseam is fine. They have a funny odor to them. I washed them once but I can still smell a faint odor. I’m going to wash them a second time hopefully the smell will go away.

Inseam is shorter than advertised. I like everything about these jeggings except that the inseam length for normal is advertised as 30.5", but it's shy of 29" which is the short size. Did the wrong tags get attached at the factory? The mistake makes these a bit too short on my 5'6" frame. I will exchange them for the long length. I'd give 5 stars if not for the length error. The fabric is substantial, stretchy, and looks like denim. The waistband hits at my bellybutton and is so thick it creates a little shaping right where it's needed, and it doesn't roll, bunch, and the jeans stay on securely. For a sexy look, I'd get a size 8, but I'm in my 50s and the 10 fits close without being tight, like dress jeans, on my 145-150 lb body. I'm delighted that these are available in different lengths!

Inconsistent Sizing I bought three of these all in size 18: dark, medium, and light washes. The dark and medium fit perfectly. But the light wash is both tighter and shorter than the other two. Luckily they are easy to return. Otherwise, the medium and dark washes fit perfectly and are a great length! I'm 5'4". The material feels durable but I'll see how they hold up after a few washes.

LOVE these jeggings! I am 5'9" and have longer legs so finding jeans that fit AND are long enough has always been a challenge. These are stretchy, nice waistband that helps with tummy control and are long enough! Plus they are super cute and look great! Never thought I would wear 'jeggings' but here I am loving these! I like to read reviews before buying an item because I am 6' tall which makes it difficult to shop for clothes. To be honest, I forgot what size I got but I think it was 10-long. They are not long to my standards - on the photo you can see that "high waist" is not up to the waist and leg length is barely enough to look ok when walking. It looks too short when sitting (also on the photo). I like the material, so that's a plus. Pants are super hard to put on because they are tight in the waist area and I barely squeeze it through my hips. Also, as a side note - while I am tall, I think I have a very proportionate body, so my legs aren't long compared to the rest of my body. I am keeping them and might even get another pare when this one is done, but there are things that I wish were better :)

Long-Legged Luxury: Perfect White Jeggings for Tall Travelers!" Finding the right fit for jeggings can be a tall order when you are 5'9" with long legs, but these white jeggings in size 6 Long were a fabulous find! I took them on a trip to Italy in March, and they were a dream come true. Not only did they fit my long legs perfectly, but they were also incredibly comfortable and flattering, making them the ideal travel companion. Worn on the stunning Amalfi Coast, they matched the beauty of the surroundings and handled everything from long walks to leisurely dinners. The jeggings maintained their shape and comfort throughout the trip, proving to be both durable and stylish. For anyone looking for great-fitting, chic, and comfortable jeggings that cater to taller frames, I can’t recommend these enough!

Great fit true to size I am 5'7" and weigh 280 lbs. (I know) I am posting some maybe not so flattering pics of my belly pouch but hopefully gives someone with same stats an idea of how these fit. First thing I absolutely loved is the color, exactly as pictured. They material is soft yet sturdy and the stretch is perfect and a nice snug..(I feel cute) fit. The waistband is super confortable.. not like a regular jean and it doesn't cut Into your waist. Important if you have a a belly and pouch like me. The length is perfect all the way down to my ankle. There was no weird smells. When I sit or bend down my backside business stays my business. They feel almost like a second skin like I could wear them all day and not even think about it. I would definitely recommend. Love the price too as I recently had gastric sleeve and am having to buy others more often I definitely will buy these at the next size down. Overall great buy for the money. Very satisfied!!

LOVE THESE I am middle-aged and all my 9/10 Old Navy jeans are too tight. I have extra weight around hips/tummy that I want to loose (hopefully), and I am not ready to give up. So meanwhile, I thought I would try a size 10 Long in these no-zip Amazon jeans. I am 5'7", and as you can see from the full leg picture, maybe I did not need the "Long". But I just now pulled them on and have not washed them yet. I would rather have a little bit of bagginess by the knees then too short at ankles. I am very satisfied with the feel and fit. It is a decent thickness of fabric --not super thin, but not thick and heavy. These are very comfortable everywhere on my crotch and legs, including the waist. The waist band seems midwaist and sits about 1.5 in below navel. Not highrise or lowrise or hipsters, etc. Some of my belly is inside the jeans, some above, but nothing feels constricted. Snug but not too much. Super comfortble when sitting and bending/squatting and my crack does not show. Hope this review helps you. I would try them out at least, since Amazon Prime gives you time to try them within the return window. I only bought one pair for now, but wow! I am excited about these. Have a blessed day.🙏💖

Color Unexpected. Otherwise Nice The color is described initially as Blush Pink. For reference I have photographed the pants on a Blush Pink quilt. Not even close to a Blush Pink. In ordering, the color is described as Coral Pink. Closer, but I would say Salmon Pink is a correct descriptor. It’s okay, Salmon Pink will work into the autumn, so maybe it’s even better. But I did order three tops to go with Blush Pink pants, and thus is disappointing. I took a star off for that reason. I ordered size 12 Long and the fit is perfect (5’7” 163 lb). It’s a thicker denim than in other leggings from Amazon Essentials, more like regular denim. Still has stretch. I think I will get a lot of wear from them.

Perfect for postpartum jeans I really like these right out of the bag. I had two babies back-to-Back and I have DR and lots of skin. The waist band on these are gentle and feel forgiving when I sit and stand. The butt fits great! Now I’m 5’7 and ordered a tall so that I can fold the bottom for cuffs and these actually have a normal length, but it also gave me a longer hip and thigh which I LOVE. It’s been why I order long pants. The demon is a great color. Need to wash before wearing because it smells like manufacturing and it’s mildly irritating to the skin. The denim is thin and has the perfect amount of stretch. I would buy these in every color! 10/10 would recommend

Not bad but I can't seem to get them in black. I purchased these pants in camel and white. I am 5'10" 222lbs and I bought them in xxl long. I love the length. I think the colors are true to pictured. The quality seems good. As you can see, I carry a lot of my weight around my middle. These pants have a wide waistband that helps suck me in a little. My only complaint is that, even with my big middle, the waist could be a little with belt loops added. They stretch out a little after wearing. I've tried to order them in black but twice I received a different pant, one being a Levi's brand, neither did I like. I'd really like to have the same pant in black. They're comfortable pants. I like them.

Stretchy yet firm I'm 5'3" 172 lbs and wear a L in most things. I have short legs so I purchased these in a 12 short. They are slightly snug at the waist, but too much looser and I'd worry about them sliding down. So far they work for me. I haven't noticed any sliding yet and the stretchiness is forgiving yet firm, so I'm not too concerned about the knees getting baggy. The back pockets are real, but not the front. I would definitely recommend these. Hopefully I still like these at the end of the day. There was a weird spot on the waist band where the stitching was done over several times. It's pretty itchy right now, maybe I'll just get used to it. Or maybe it rubs my stomach raw and I curse Amazon as I'm putting bandaids on the spot. only time will tell.

comfortable & attractive pull-on jeans, good value for the price The fabric these jeggings are made of is thick enough to look like real jeans, but just a soft and stretchy as leggings. Way better quality than most jeggings. I got the black and the dark wash and I really like both of them. I love having no button to cut into my tummy when I sit, or bulky zipper under my shirts and sweaters. I'm 5'2" and 110lb and the 0 fits snugly but isn't too tight to sit cross legged. The 0 is likely long enough to cover your ankles if you are up to 5'6" or so, but for shorter legs it's easy to just tuck the extra fabric under for a hemmed look. These are likely to become my go-to jeans to wear with sneakers or boots. I wish they made them in boot cut, too!

Good fit and comfortable, but created a muffin top I ordered the short size 12 and they are good but not the ideal jegging for me but still worth keeping. I have taken two photos with two different shirts and you can see that with the loose shirt, it camouflages my waist area. With the tighter shirt, it created an unflattering fit to see my fat bulging from the tight waistband. Ideally a looser band would be preferred, but I have a difficult body to fit jeans and pants since I am 5’3” and I have more of a “gut” than butt. So to get pants to fit in the butt, I have to order a size down but then it creates a tight waist and sometimes a muffin top and this is exactly what these jeggings did for me. But other than that, the length is great, the material nice and stretchy while still looking like jeans, they are comfy, and the waist band is still comfortable, despite the muffin top effect. I think I’m going to keep and just wear with looser shirts.

Great quality I thought these were a good quality, nice pair of jeggings. That being said they did not look good on my body type ( plus size) it made my stomach area look so unflattering but if you wear a long shirt to cover that area they look nice. I decided to return because they did nothing for my figure at all, I don’t expect magic but I also don’t want it to make it look worse than it is. I am sure this look great on women with more of a flat tummy area. For reference I am 5’6 and 210lbs and a size 16 and ordered the 16 and fit great. Very true to size in my opinion.

Great value and nice enough to wear in public I liked these palazzo pants so much I ordered a second pair in black and white polka dot. They are soft and drape nicely from the hip. They are a true palazzo pant, which I like, but buyers should be aware that they are "flowy." I am 5'6" and weigh 155 lbs. I normally wear an XL but got the XXL as prompted by Amazon. Great, but inconsistent sizing Originally ordered M of the gray with roses when I was 4 mo pregnant, and they were the only thing that felt comfortable to wear. Once I got to six months pregnant, I ordered the large in the same color. However, the waistband of the large was 1 inch smaller than the waistband of the medium! (See pic. The medium is the wider pair on the bottom.) I still loved the pants, so I decided to return the large and order an extra large in a different color, the khaki with roses. I figured maybe in a different color they would actually be larger. I received the khaki ones today, and the fit is what I would’ve expected the large to be, but they are see-through. So now I am trying the gray ones again, this time in xxl, hoping at some point the larger sizes actually get bigger. Apart from the size inconsistencies, these are the comfiest lounge pants, and I highly recommend them for pregnant women! They are silky smooth and feel great against tender stretched belly skin. For size comparison, my pre-preggo size in most clothes is S/M. 5’6, 142lb, hourglass. Now I’m 166lb at 6mo pregnant. The mediums still fit, but I anticipate needing a size larger as I get closer to due date.

Best Pants for C Section Surgery and just all around comfortable lounge pants! I love these lounge pants! I'm currently 36 weeks pregnant, and with just 2 weeks to go until my C section, I've been living in these. I originally ordered 2 pairs, but I just ordered a 3rd because after my surgery I know I'll need loose pants that don't constrict my incision, and I have a feeling I'll only be wearing these at home for the first few weeks of recovery. I wear a size medium before and during pregnancy. I'm only 5' 3", so these do run a bit long on me. But I only wear them around the house with slippers so I don't mind one bit. With how loose fitting these are, I have no doubt I could wear a size small but I'm fine with them. They're loose enough that they don't constrict my waist, but I can also pull them about halfway up my preggo belly and they'll fit there comfortably, which I really need for after my C section. I wish I'd bought these for my first baby during my surgery recovery, and i highly recommend them before, during, and after having a baby even if you don't plan to have a C section. The weight I've gained during this pregnancy seems to be all baby, so I know I'll still fit comfortably in these after the baby comes. I may have to roll up the waist though. Highly recommend!

Cute and comfy These pants are good to lounge around the house. PJ material and comfy. They are a cheap material, but that's ok due to how inexpensive they are. I don't see them lasting long tho, but time will tell. I noticed many said these are super long. I’m not sure if they changed the length on some styles, but the camo pair I got wasn’t very long for my height of 5’5. I'm wearing a size small. I don't see this being a good length on someone taller than my height. At least not this style. However, I do have long legs tho, so that may play a role as well. A little tip for people saying the string comes out when washed. Tie a small knot in the string before washing and it won’t come out. It’s easy to take the knot out after washing. Also, it's probably best to hang dry for longer wear, since it is a cheaper material. I’m middle age (52), and they are good if you get hot flashes, since the material is light. Hope this helps someone.

Different patterns= Very different types of fabric First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

My normal size M/L at 8 months pregnant I got the 3XL There are a lot of these same looking pants sold all over Amazon. I ordered some by the brand LUBERLIN and I definitely think the fit of these AMiERY ones are better. Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

Great pants in all sizes! Not see through! I love these pants! I bought them for my sisters and mother, a variety of sizes and body types. They fit all of us and I just bought the sizes I would regularly assume we wear. In the photo the sizes I bought and our measurements are listed from left to right; size small (she is 5’2 and 120 lbs), size xxl (5’8 and 260lbs), size medium (5’5 and 160), and me wearing a medium (5’4 and 145). One of us had a full pair of black pants underneath in this photo and at least one other was wearing black underwear and you can’t tell, which is awesome! They are long on all of us, and there is a large waist band, the second photo shows me with the waist pulled up all the way covering my belly button and meeting my bottom rib, I probably could have gone with a smaller size as they are quite loose and really stretchy but I’m happy all the same :) the fabric is cool, I can cuddle my dog and roll around with him and they don’t get hairy 🙏🏻.

Not the best material, but very comfy and stretchy I have purchased 3 pairs of these over the course of the past few years and I’ll probably continue to in the future. They’re definitely not of the highest quality, but all 3 pairs have held up so far and are fantastic for lounging around the house. It’s such a huge bonus for me that they have worked for pregnancy and non-pregnancy wear, without stretching out. I’ve noticed these are generally true to size as well. I love the length, being that i am 5’ 7” with long legs. The pictures are of my most recent purchase; I’m about 33 weeks pregnant, roughly a size 10/12 in regular sizes at this point, and I’m wearing a size large of these pants. I probably could’ve sized up for a looser fit, but I found these comfy and not restricting at all. Love them!

Comfy, high-waisted, thin material - perfect house pants! I really love these pants. The material is thin and somewhat stretchy, and they are very comfortable! I'd be happy wearing these around the house any time of year (in California). I was looking for some comfortable lounge pants to wear at home and was delighted to find such cute ones! The high waist is fine, not my favorite, but I love the colors and the length is great on me. I'm 5'8" and about 145 lbs and I got a large, and they are a perfect fit. Now I can be lazy in style with these cute, comfy pants! I might just need some more pairs...Edit: Five years later I still wear and love these pants! I don't wear them every day but I wear them plenty and they have held up nicely. I have since had a baby and put on some weight and now weigh about 170 lbs, but I bought another pair in large last week and it's as comfy as ever! I love these pants so much!

Nice quality, but fit long as expected Purchased for lounging around the house or as pajama pants. They're very comfortable! The material feels soft, and feel thick enough that patterned or bright colored underwear might not be too noticeable through the white stripes. I bought a size small, I'm 5'4" w/ an average build and they are long for me as I expected. Although the waist is adjustable, the fit in the waist is fine, but I will have to fold the waist band over for a better fit. Even folded these are a couple inches too long for me. But I am pleased with the quality! I do wish an x-small size was available!! Purchased a second pair with a different design, just as happy!

Waist is good, long in length I'm a size 12/14 or XL usually in regular clothes. I ordered a size XXXL based on their chart and reviews. I like them and wear them plenty. I could have gotten away with a XXL, but I'm perfectly fine in a 3XL as well; they're not falling down or anything if I tie the string. I'm 5'7" and these are looooong. I can wear them with flats, but they have to be pulled up a bit high on my waist (they do have a decent length crotch too though so you don't get a camel toe when you do that lol) You can dress these up or down. I wear them just about everywhere. Pair with a sheer blouse for work or with a tank top for the store. Very silky and light enough for summer in the mid-south. I got 2 colors and will be buying more.

Comfortable and beautiful! I have been seeing pants like these for several months on Pinterest and they just seemed too good to be true. Cute, affordable, and comfy- could I really have stumbled upon the trifecta of pants? I did! I am 5’7” and as I’m on the curvier side and have long legs, I ordered the XL. They fit perfectly and the pattern looks just like the pictures. I know they’re intended to be more of a lounge pant, but the print is so beautiful I think I want to dress them up. With a fitted top and nice sandal these might be cute dresses up. They’re so light they’ll do well in warm or cool spring or summer weather. I hope they hold up. I’ll likely wash them in cold on the delicate cycle and line dry. If they survive their first few washes, I’ll be buying these for every woman I know.

Love! For some reason, my review keeps defaulting to the pants I bought a while back. I've included a pic of the ones I'm reviewing here, the camo stars/stripes.I will always buy Amery brand. Everything I've bought from them has been perfect. These pants are so gorgeous, they fit great, and are extremely comfy. As you can see in the pic, they're not a jet black/stark white, but more of a muted color. That's fine, because I love them so much. I bought a large because I like everything to be loose. I'm 3'7" and 130 lbs and am very glad I got the large because they fit perfect. I will buy more of these, even though I have 6 or 7 pairs already. Great purchase!

Perfect fit, extremely soft, very thin Perfect fit, SO SOFT!! Bought the gray striped version in 3X (24 in the US). It loses a star only because it's very thin material that shows a bit too much. Also, I'm 5'3" and they brush the ground. I love everything just a little long so perfect length for me. That said, I've purchased other prints to try them out. 2 more patterns of this item: All are the same thin-but-soft material. -- Black stripe is just like the gray but immediately lost the string from the waist upon first wash. -- Navy floral same plus picture attached. I put my thumb straight through the seam on first wear after about 5 hours. I have another pair but have not worn them yet. They lost the drawstring in the wash as well (I even made sure to tie them before washing, I think the material is just too "slippery". Down to 3 stars now.

Love super long pants and arrived a day later Love these pants. Exactly what I wanted. I love super long pants. I ordered a 2x I'm usually an xL so I went up a size and they fit perfect. I'm 5 foot 3 and 185lbs. A lil long but I'm sure they'll shrink a little and I like my pants dragging on the floor lol. I ordered a black pair but didn't try them on yet. I normally don't like this material but its soft, comfortable and flattering. They ferl like jersey sheets and doesn't appear to be see through. Would buy again. Update, washed it and did not shrink but out of 2 pairs of these pants, the string came out of one of the pairs in the wash

The pants do run large, though. I'm a 6 in pants and usually size up for PJ's, but the medium swallowed me. The small is still a little big, but fine for PJs. When sizing down from a medium to a small, the pants lost a few inches in length. I'm almost 5'8", and the medium was quite long (I loved it, but the length might not be for everyone). The small is a fine length for my height, though I wouldn't want them to be any shorter. Overall, great PJs, just go down a size!

They don't look like a pajama bottom and you could easily wear them to run errands, grab lunch etc. Highly recommend!

Great, but inconsistent sizing Originally ordered M of the gray with roses when I was 4 mo pregnant, and they were the only thing that felt comfortable to wear. Once I got to six months pregnant, I ordered the large in the same color. However, the waistband of the large was 1 inch smaller than the waistband of the medium! (See pic. The medium is the wider pair on the bottom.) I still loved the pants, so I decided to return the large and order an extra large in a different color, the khaki with roses. I figured maybe in a different color they would actually be larger. I received the khaki ones today, and the fit is what I would’ve expected the large to be, but they are see-through. So now I am trying the gray ones again, this time in xxl, hoping at some point the larger sizes actually get bigger. Apart from the size inconsistencies, these are the comfiest lounge pants, and I highly recommend them for pregnant women! They are silky smooth and feel great against tender stretched belly skin. For size comparison, my pre-preggo size in most clothes is S/M. 5’6, 142lb, hourglass. Now I’m 166lb at 6mo pregnant. The mediums still fit, but I anticipate needing a size larger as I get closer to due date.

Best Pants for C Section Surgery and just all around comfortable lounge pants! I love these lounge pants! I'm currently 36 weeks pregnant, and with just 2 weeks to go until my C section, I've been living in these. I originally ordered 2 pairs, but I just ordered a 3rd because after my surgery I know I'll need loose pants that don't constrict my incision, and I have a feeling I'll only be wearing these at home for the first few weeks of recovery. I wear a size medium before and during pregnancy. I'm only 5' 3", so these do run a bit long on me. But I only wear them around the house with slippers so I don't mind one bit. With how loose fitting these are, I have no doubt I could wear a size small but I'm fine with them. They're loose enough that they don't constrict my waist, but I can also pull them about halfway up my preggo belly and they'll fit there comfortably, which I really need for after my C section. I wish I'd bought these for my first baby during my surgery recovery, and i highly recommend them before, during, and after having a baby even if you don't plan to have a C section. The weight I've gained during this pregnancy seems to be all baby, so I know I'll still fit comfortably in these after the baby comes. I may have to roll up the waist though. Highly recommend!

Size up I'm 6ft tall 215lbs I have a nice foundation type body... my legs are long AF I GOT THE XL my first ones and they were to short... my second ones I got 2xl and they still ran a little flood but the 3xl are perfect in length... I'm a size 12 in jeans and these are soft and flowy and I freaking love them in the 3xl.... the xl fit me perfectly in size except length so I sized up and they're perfect for lounging and looking cute

Cute and comfy These pants are good to lounge around the house. PJ material and comfy. They are a cheap material, but that's ok due to how inexpensive they are. I don't see them lasting long tho, but time will tell. I noticed many said these are super long. I’m not sure if they changed the length on some styles, but the camo pair I got wasn’t very long for my height of 5’5. I'm wearing a size small. I don't see this being a good length on someone taller than my height. At least not this style. However, I do have long legs tho, so that may play a role as well. A little tip for people saying the string comes out when washed. Tie a small knot in the string before washing and it won’t come out. It’s easy to take the knot out after washing. Also, it's probably best to hang dry for longer wear, since it is a cheaper material. I’m middle age (52), and they are good if you get hot flashes, since the material is light. Hope this helps someone.

Different patterns= Very different types of fabric First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

My normal size M/L at 8 months pregnant I got the 3XL There are a lot of these same looking pants sold all over Amazon. I ordered some by the brand LUBERLIN and I definitely think the fit of these AMiERY ones are better. Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

Great pants in all sizes! Not see through! I love these pants! I bought them for my sisters and mother, a variety of sizes and body types. They fit all of us and I just bought the sizes I would regularly assume we wear. In the photo the sizes I bought and our measurements are listed from left to right; size small (she is 5’2 and 120 lbs), size xxl (5’8 and 260lbs), size medium (5’5 and 160), and me wearing a medium (5’4 and 145). One of us had a full pair of black pants underneath in this photo and at least one other was wearing black underwear and you can’t tell, which is awesome! They are long on all of us, and there is a large waist band, the second photo shows me with the waist pulled up all the way covering my belly button and meeting my bottom rib, I probably could have gone with a smaller size as they are quite loose and really stretchy but I’m happy all the same :) the fabric is cool, I can cuddle my dog and roll around with him and they don’t get hairy 🙏🏻.

Not the best material, but very comfy and stretchy I have purchased 3 pairs of these over the course of the past few years and I’ll probably continue to in the future. They’re definitely not of the highest quality, but all 3 pairs have held up so far and are fantastic for lounging around the house. It’s such a huge bonus for me that they have worked for pregnancy and non-pregnancy wear, without stretching out. I’ve noticed these are generally true to size as well. I love the length, being that i am 5’ 7” with long legs. The pictures are of my most recent purchase; I’m about 33 weeks pregnant, roughly a size 10/12 in regular sizes at this point, and I’m wearing a size large of these pants. I probably could’ve sized up for a looser fit, but I found these comfy and not restricting at all. Love them!

Comfy, high-waisted, thin material - perfect house pants! I really love these pants. The material is thin and somewhat stretchy, and they are very comfortable! I'd be happy wearing these around the house any time of year (in California). I was looking for some comfortable lounge pants to wear at home and was delighted to find such cute ones! The high waist is fine, not my favorite, but I love the colors and the length is great on me. I'm 5'8" and about 145 lbs and I got a large, and they are a perfect fit. Now I can be lazy in style with these cute, comfy pants! I might just need some more pairs...Edit: Five years later I still wear and love these pants! I don't wear them every day but I wear them plenty and they have held up nicely. I have since had a baby and put on some weight and now weigh about 170 lbs, but I bought another pair in large last week and it's as comfy as ever! I love these pants so much!

Nice quality, but fit long as expected Purchased for lounging around the house or as pajama pants. They're very comfortable! The material feels soft, and feel thick enough that patterned or bright colored underwear might not be too noticeable through the white stripes. I bought a size small, I'm 5'4" w/ an average build and they are long for me as I expected. Although the waist is adjustable, the fit in the waist is fine, but I will have to fold the waist band over for a better fit. Even folded these are a couple inches too long for me. But I am pleased with the quality! I do wish an x-small size was available!! Purchased a second pair with a different design, just as happy!

Waist is good, long in length I'm a size 12/14 or XL usually in regular clothes. I ordered a size XXXL based on their chart and reviews. I like them and wear them plenty. I could have gotten away with a XXL, but I'm perfectly fine in a 3XL as well; they're not falling down or anything if I tie the string. I'm 5'7" and these are looooong. I can wear them with flats, but they have to be pulled up a bit high on my waist (they do have a decent length crotch too though so you don't get a camel toe when you do that lol) You can dress these up or down. I wear them just about everywhere. Pair with a sheer blouse for work or with a tank top for the store. Very silky and light enough for summer in the mid-south. I got 2 colors and will be buying more.

Comfortable and beautiful! I have been seeing pants like these for several months on Pinterest and they just seemed too good to be true. Cute, affordable, and comfy- could I really have stumbled upon the trifecta of pants? I did! I am 5’7” and as I’m on the curvier side and have long legs, I ordered the XL. They fit perfectly and the pattern looks just like the pictures. I know they’re intended to be more of a lounge pant, but the print is so beautiful I think I want to dress them up. With a fitted top and nice sandal these might be cute dresses up. They’re so light they’ll do well in warm or cool spring or summer weather. I hope they hold up. I’ll likely wash them in cold on the delicate cycle and line dry. If they survive their first few washes, I’ll be buying these for every woman I know.

Love! For some reason, my review keeps defaulting to the pants I bought a while back. I've included a pic of the ones I'm reviewing here, the camo stars/stripes.I will always buy Amery brand. Everything I've bought from them has been perfect. These pants are so gorgeous, they fit great, and are extremely comfy. As you can see in the pic, they're not a jet black/stark white, but more of a muted color. That's fine, because I love them so much. I bought a large because I like everything to be loose. I'm 3'7" and 130 lbs and am very glad I got the large because they fit perfect. I will buy more of these, even though I have 6 or 7 pairs already. Great purchase!

Perfect fit, extremely soft, very thin Perfect fit, SO SOFT!! Bought the gray striped version in 3X (24 in the US). It loses a star only because it's very thin material that shows a bit too much. Also, I'm 5'3" and they brush the ground. I love everything just a little long so perfect length for me. That said, I've purchased other prints to try them out. 2 more patterns of this item: All are the same thin-but-soft material. -- Black stripe is just like the gray but immediately lost the string from the waist upon first wash. -- Navy floral same plus picture attached. I put my thumb straight through the seam on first wear after about 5 hours. I have another pair but have not worn them yet. They lost the drawstring in the wash as well (I even made sure to tie them before washing, I think the material is just too "slippery". Down to 3 stars now.

Love super long pants and arrived a day later Love these pants. Exactly what I wanted. I love super long pants. I ordered a 2x I'm usually an xL so I went up a size and they fit perfect. I'm 5 foot 3 and 185lbs. A lil long but I'm sure they'll shrink a little and I like my pants dragging on the floor lol. I ordered a black pair but didn't try them on yet. I normally don't like this material but its soft, comfortable and flattering. They ferl like jersey sheets and doesn't appear to be see through. Would buy again. These are so cute and comfortable!!! I knew they would be slightly shorter than the picture because of my height, but still perfect for lounging and still very cute. So nice for travel and hot summer weather! I got size Medium, color Beige. I'm 5'5, 145lbs, waist 32, hips 41. I probably could've sized up to a Large for a better fit, and a little more length. I was hesitant to get these, but I needed something light to wear during a recent work trip to Bahamas. I wanted to look put together, while still being comfortable. I wore these on the plane, and I was very comfortable - not too hot or cold. I also wore them around the resort where it was hot and humid, and again, I was super comfortable! I was worried about my mom belly being emphasized, but the loose linen material helped to camouflage it! For a sleeker look, I did wear shape wear, but it really wasn't needed. They're somewhat sheer, I wore shape wear underneath and didn't have any issues with anything showing through. The pockets are deep, and they don't look too bulky when you put items in them. I carried my phone, a small wallet, and lipgloss, and my phone was really the only noticeable item in my pocket. They look cute with sneakers or sandals. I'm sure they'd look cute with heels, too. Note - At first they felt too short and frumpy, but I just adjusted how they sat on my hips and waist until I was happy. I think a t-shirt with a front tuck or knot looked really cute. They do wrinkle, but spritz them with a little water, and the wrinkles will shake right out. I took one star off because they do feel slightly rough, and they're dry-clean only.

Breezy linen with long legs These pants are a pleasant surprise! The linen is heavier than expected for a good drape and no show through; at least not in the khaki color. The styling is long and flowy, albeit longer than expected. In the attached photos, the pants haven’t been washed and dried yet, but they fall over my feet; even in platform flip flops. I’m just over 5’5” and ordered size XL. Taller wearers will likely be pleased with the length. The waistband is wide and stretchy, even just a touch snug. Since these pants are heavier gauge linen, I think I could get away with them this fall paired with boots and a sweater. Versatility is never a bad thing!

Super Cute - Dry Clean Only! I tried these on right out of the package and they are ADORABLE! I'm 5'6, 125, and they fit perfectly, just a little baggy like they're supposed to be. However... So cute and comfortable These loose pants are so versatile they could take you from relaxing at home to the beach to a night out with friends. They are loose and flowy, so much so that I ordered a size down and they fit perfectly. The fully elasticized waist and the length are just right for me (I am 5'6" tall). The 70% rayon/30% linen blend feels light and cool. There are two deep pockets that add to the style while performing a practical function; they are big enough to hold a phone, a wallet, lipstick or whatever you may need. The long braided ties at the waist are not functional, but they look cute and are a little dressier looking than most pants' drawstrings. These are my new favorite pants and I will be wearing them A LOT!

Shrunk alot First, the color is beautiful and I’m sad these aren’t going to work. They are a good weight, more heavier than I expected but in a good way. They fit good before I washed them. I can’t wear them now that they’ve shrunk.

Easy, Breezy, Comfy & Cool As a plus-size girly it is HARD to find palazzo pants that fit and also flow. But these are the ones! I love these pants so much that I bought two more pairs (chocolate & black) in the same size. I’ve worn them to church, date night, around the house, and running errands. They are as soft as your favorite sleep shirt but not see through. Flowy enough that you won’t get hot in the summer but don’t show panty lines. The ruching across the midsection is strong enough to pull high to natural waist or down around your hips and it’ll stay there. Some slight pilling after several washes but nothing big enough to be bothersome. (And my only other complaint is that, while your cell WILL fit in the pockets, excess weight WILL pull the waistband down a bit. Not all the way, just feels weird). If you are looking for breezy summer trousers, buy these. Edit: I am 5’8” and weigh 275lbs

The elasticated waist is so comfortable and I’m pretty sure someone at least two sizes smaller or larger than me (usually a 2XL) could wear them easily due to the way the waist is sewn. Size DOWN at least 1 size! 5'2" - 165-170 lbs - 41"-36"-41" - White is lined! Hot weather approved. TLDR — Size down at least 1 size, if not 2. Length is good for some petites (5'1" to 5'3") and regular height (up to 5'7"). I'm 5'2" and weigh between 165-170 lbs. depending on the time of the month. My measurements are 41" Bust - 36" Waist - 41" Hips. I generally wear size 10-12 or L-XL depending on the brand, fabric, type of clothing, etc. I have a small FUPA but no butt, so occasionally I can size down to a size 6-8 or M (Medium) depending on the stretch of the fabric. I'm short-waisted and usually wear mid- or high-rise with a short or regular inseam depending on the pants and type of shoes I'm wearing. I ordered size L (Large) as suggested by Amazon in the color white, and it's too loose and baggy overall. The smocked waistband is comfortable and super stretchy. The white linen pants have lining inside all the way down except 6" from the bottom so it's not see through! You can't see the deep pockets... maybe very faintly if you're looking really hard in the picture. (I wish other white pants are lined like this, because there is nothing more annoying than having white pants that are practically transparent where you can see the pockets or even your nude underwear.) Because the pants are lined, it does add some noticeable weight to the overall feel of the pants, and along with the super stretchy smocked waistband, it causes them to slowly slide down. The pockets are deep and a great addition, and you could fit a phone in there, but because of the overall "heaviness" of the fabric and lining, the phone would definitely pull down the pants because the waistband won't support it. For this reason, I would size down to a size Medium for a relaxed fit or even a Small for a tighter fit. I'm only 5'2" and have the smocked waistband pulled above my belly button. The length of these pants are 41.5" with an inseam of 27.5". I'm wearing 2" platform sandals where the pants are almost grazing the floor. If you're shorter than 5'2" and don't want to wear 3" heels/ wedges/ platform sandals, these may be too long for you. Check the size chart for the overall length of the pants. According to the tag, this is made in China and it's only 30% linen and 70% rayon. It says, "Dry clean only. Do not bleach. Steam iron." I used a garment steamer to get the wrinkles out. The fabric isn't scratchy like typical linen since this isn't 100% linen. It felt light and breathable, even in hot, humid 92°F weather. Sitting on them obviously causes them to wrinkle in the back but the front remains mostly wrinkle free. Based on the size chart, I think going by the hip measurement would be best. Size medium for me would provide a much better fit where the waistband is hopefully a little tighter but the pants are still relaxed enough, while size small would provide an overall tighter fit. I'm debating on washing these to shrink them down a little as others have mentioned. Aside from the dry cleaning care instructions, who has time for that?! I do like these pants a lot and will order another pair or two in different colors and smaller sizes. I'll update my review later if I remember.

Perfect travel, dress-up/dress down pant. Better than shorts for a classier look. Only thing that helped me survive 95 degree temps in Europe this summer. So loose and breathable, I bought 9 colors! I was so comfortable and it kept the hot sun off my legs. I'm 5'1", 116 lbs. I hemmed them a bit and after washing them in cold and line drying them, they still want to shrink. Stretching them back into shape while wet seems to do the trick. In fact the only reason for four stars instead of five is that the care instructions are not clear that it should be dry clean only. So my recommendation is as if you need to hem them, wash them first and then see how much you need to take off after. If you're tall and they fit you perfectly out of the package then I would definitely just dry clean them.

SUPER CUTE SWEATS! I ordered these ANRABESS Women Wide Leg Baggy Cargo Sweatpants in a size Small for my 10 year old daughter and she loves the way they fit! For reference, she's 5' tall and 105lbs-ish with a 26" waist. Though these pants are a little long on her, she insists that is fashion... and I'm here for it! Here's what we like about these sweatpants: •QUALITY GARMENT: These sweats are well made. The stitching is straight and secure. They feel more like a higher end pair of sweatpants than fast fashion. •SOFT: These sweatpants are made of very soft, cozy material. While they are warm, they aren't extremely thick or heavily lined with fleece. •FASHION FORWARD: The actual cut of these pants is really nice! They are very baggy in the legs, specifically at the knees. But they taper up at the waist, giving them a really good shape. My daughter loves the cargo pockets on the sides. •WASH & WEAR! These pants can be washed, dried, and worn dozens of times without compromising the quality. Mom win! Overall, my daughter is just obsessed with these! She likes to wear them with tank tops and a bunch of accessories (not shown in the picture, but she was just lounging). She also wears them with graphic tees and athletic shoes. I just like that they are affordable, comfortable ad made well. We highly recommend them!

nice and comfortable, super flowy! I ordered a large based on the size chart, and found the pants to fit well. I normally wear a size 10, and prefer my pants to have at least a 30 inch waist to fit comfortably. These were spot on and while medium would've been fine, the large gave me the nice "paper bag" pant look with the rouching at the top. I'm 5'5" and found the inseem really worked nicely without being too short (they're not as short as they appear on the model, unless maybe you hike them up more?). The fabric is medium weight, and is actually a bit ribbed with a pronounced seem in the front (not the back). They did come a bit wrinkled, so I'm not sure how they'll hold up when traveling, although that is what I got them for. They're so comfy though so I'll give it a try on my next flight and see what happens! They feel like my pajama pants, so will be ideal for overnight flights while looking chic. I got the dark khaki and they are actually a bit darker brown, and even a bit greenish than the photo shows. Overall I'm super happy, particularly for the price!

THESE ARE A YES, but.. FINAL UPDATE: The seller reached out to me about the year in the leggings. A replacement was sent free of charge and arrived very timely. Have not had any problems with the replacement or the other pairs I have. Still recommend to friends and family & have plans to buy more! Love how they feel and look! 🥳 UPDATE: I’ve had the leggings for about a month and a half and the teal ones are, unfortunately, ripping right now the middle. I’ve really loved them and the others are fine for now - but it’s really disappointing that they haven’t lasted long. I’ve only air dried them and wash them on a soft cycle. ________________________________________ To preface: I am 145lbs, 5’8, bigger in the hips area with most of my height being in my legs. I ordered 5 pairs of these so far - 3 originally, 2 more a few days after. The first pairs ordered were: Forest Green Marl, Bright Red Marl and the Charcoal, size S. I had to return the red because they were more see through than preferred (which was actually my fault. I didn’t read the provided info about that color being less squat proof). Overall, the fit is good! They were quite compressed and both the Forest and Charcoal were squat proof. Because of my long legs, both were about 2 inches above my ankles, which I don’t mind. Neither of them showed sweat for me, but my butt doesn’t really sweat much so... it’s hard to say for sure on that one. The two additional pairs purchased were: Indigo and Wine Red Marl, size M. (Different size mainly only becuse I was curious how they would fit compared to the S). They fit very similarly to the S! Still compressed, just slightly less, and still stopped about 2 inches above my ankles. However, the wine red were a little bit shorter than the other 3 pairs, both in how far above my ankles they stopped and even just holding them up to the other pairs. These ones stopped about 5 inches above my ankles, which I don’t really mind enough to return because they are still great leggings, just a bit shorter than the others - hence, the 4 stars instead of 5. Otherwise, everything else was great. My return of the bright red pants was very simple and quick and am getting a full refund for them. I can also say, I teaterred for like 5 hours on what size I should get. The S and M both fit, the only difference is that the S fit a little bit tighter.

THESE ARE MY NEW FAVORITE LEGGINGS - FROM PICKIEST PERSON EVER To start, I am almost 5'9 and about 145 lbs. I wear a size 7-9 for pants. I got a size medium in charcoal grey and these seriously could not have fit any better. First of all, I have been looking for the perfect pair of leggings for YEARS. Leggings seem to always either slip down or stretch out while working out, have too much fabric around the crotch, have an uncomfortable/unflattering waistband, are see-through, etc. Ladies, you understand all these issues. These leggings were so great that I am 100% convinced that I will buy a pair in every color and throw all of my current leggings away. All of the issues I listed above are nonexistent with these leggings. The waistband is not lined so it doesn't give unflattering shape and it goes above the bellybutton very comfortably. These leggings have the PERFECT amount of stretch and DO NOT STRETCH OUT while working out which seems to always be an issue. They contour to your body amazingly and have those details that make your butt look perfect. They are super comfy. The fact that I got the grey colored leggings and they barely show any sweat is unreal. I have worn these heavy lifting, to cycle class, and more and they have definitely held up with any exercise. I have already washed them a couple of times (on delicate/gentle heat) and they still are good as new. Any way, this is my first and probably last review of anything I have ever bought from anywhere but I just had to share how much I love these leggings because I know there are lots of other women out there who were just as frustrated with finding the perfect leggings as I was. For $27, you cannot do any better.

Some great and okay qualities! Just received the product today. I got a size small and I'm 5'2.5" 128 Ibs size 27" waist. I did have to size down from my typical medium size. I own a few gymshark leggings. These fit just right, 7/8 length and comes right above my belly button. The elastic part of the waist is nice and compressive however I wish the rest of the material was the same way. Right below the elastic my lower abdomen tends to pop out a little more because the rest of the leggings are not compressive like the waist. The material itself is a little thinner than what I had expected, but it is soft and not uncomfortable. Because of this, I'm not sure how well these leggings will hold up in the washer, weightlifting and core exercises. I will try a few washes and wears and give my second opinion after. Futher, the small dotted holes are not actually holes, they are darker dots printed onto the pants. Overall, fit is great and accentuates the butt area. Very flattering! For the price, I did expect a slightly thicker material with a bit more compression -just because there are other leggings on Amazon that are <$29. If you are not looking for too much compression or looking to wear these for light training, highly recommend. Rated 3/5 for now because of material, if they hold up well in the long run will give 4/5 rating! UPDATE: decided to return these. material for the front end is too thin for me, and gives you camel toe. UPDATE 2: i was contacted by the seller. I received a second pair from a list of suggestions (i got khaki grey marl) and was told that these would be more compressive and have less camel toe. Upon trying them on, they were more compressive but they still did have camel toe. I suspect that even though i like the tighter fit I should size up to a medium to avoid camel toe. Otherwise, great customer service, seller seems to really care about its customers!! 4/5 stars.

Size comparison: M vs L Squat Proof. These leggings are soft, a good mix between an align and a wunder under. Good for the gym, walks, pilates etc but I got them for the gym. Now I’m 5’5, 170 and I carry my weight moreso in my lower half. Usually a medium across the board, sometimes in strechier leggings I can do a small. I initially ordered these in a M Blueberry and they felt tight! I wanted good compression for a flattering fit but these felt a little too tight so I ordered a L. I was also bloated the first day I tried them so I figured I’d just compare. The L came and they were sooo soft, so comfortable that I could probably take a nap in them. Not what you typically want in a gym legging lol. They had less compression and I noticed neither pair completely smoothed me out in the front. The M fit a little better on this day since I was less bloated but still somewhat bloated. They felt tight but I figured they were a way better fit between the two. The L also flattened my booty and the M made it look great, I guess the compression did a little lifting in that area. I ended up returning the L right away since they are definitely too big, and I’m debating returning the M too because maybe these are just not for me? I want something that smooths and flatters and the compression on these wasn’t as much as I was hoping for. The waistband on both pairs was restricting so if you’re expecting or TTC, these will not grow with the bump much despite the legs being super stretchy just FYI. Both waistbands rolled down a little when I was trying them on, especially with the L, but I don’t think that’s a deal breaker for weight lifting and light cardio. Would not recommend for running.

Highly Recommend 8/23/22: I am 5' 7.75", average 160lb, 30" waist, 41.5" hips. It was hard to determine if these were going to be as high as I like them. I have a longer torso than most but they ended up being perfect. One picture I'm pointing to my bellybutton for reference. I bought the 'storm green' and 'black' in Medium. They both fit the same. The compression is great; not too much, not too little. The fabric is also buttery soft. They are MUCH easier to slide over the heel than the BB ones. These aren't exactly sweat proof but they do dry pretty quickly. They both appeared squat proof. Saw reviews that the top was rolling down (I believe it's due to them being too small), I didn't have this issue. Will buy more and different colors! 8/31/22: bought 'Steel Blue' and 'Navy', would give them the same quality and rating as the black and green initially bought. 10/1/22: I asked for 2 pairs of black for a birthday gift. I deducted star because of these. They are different, both the feel of them and fit of them. They are slightly tighter and the fabric is less soft (it's a fabric that lint clings to more than the initial ones I received). I didn't find a need to return them because it's still good quality for the price, I just like them less than the first 4 pair.

Quality, Practical, Flattering I am 5'7", 165lbs, long legs and torso. Usually wear medium in shorts and leggings in other brands like gymshark but size up to a large for this brand but I might consider a medium next time to help them stay up better. I have these in the bike short version and they are my favorite shorts to wear under my uniform for Brazilian Jiu-jitsu because they stay in place and comfortable. After a year and mannny washings they are still holding up perfectly! These leggings are long enough have a perfect high-rise that is slightly higher in the back so there is no gapping. If you wanted a lower rise you can fold down the waistband. They have no front seam and have a long crotch gusset, so full range of movement, no wedgies/camel toe. The fabric is very soft and stretchy and not sheer at all so definitely squat proof. They fit like a second skin, not too much compression but keep their shape and elasticity over long term use. I only have them in black and they do not show sweat much. I love them for lifting weights and for just lounging around. Definitely recommending to my friends and family! This brand has great knock offs!

Great fit and a really good dupe I’m usually between XS and Small with 26.5” waist and 36” hips. The XS in Storm Green fit perfectly. The color is perfection. Squat proof and cellulite friendly. They are compressive and soft, but slightly more of a cotton than brushed feel compared to the BB original. While they do still collect lint/per fur it brushes off more easily than the softer/more brushed original. The details, which can impact the fit, are what differ. In the photos the Black is BB and the Green is Aox Jox—both XS. The Aox Jox has a lower rise (by almost 1.5 inches), but does come up almost as high in the back. I have a short torso, so this meant a better fit and less rolling in the dupe. Another difference is the seams. The Aox Jox version has four total detailing seams and the top actually touches the waist band in the back. The Aox Jox also has less tapering at the waist so about an inch more room at the waist than the BB version. Aox Jox also has more room at the ankle for sliding over your heel. Overall, aside from the BB version is softer, but that’s the only winning category. Factor in price and this dupe is an absolute buy.

The underline is white but, it seems to be squat proof. I've already worn this six times and have washed them six times (air dried) and it's still holding up (I do a lot of heavy squats and deadlifts). I'm also very sweaty and the picture is after two hours at the gym, no sign of sweaty stains. Will be buying another pair!

Where have these been?! So i was skeptical but damn these fit nice. They feel nice, no pulling up the waistband constantly as nice moderate compression. I'm hooked! Hopefully they make it thru the wash and if so I'm stocking up! For reference I'm 5'9", 168# usually m/l in legging, 8/10 in most pants, athletic build. **Update** I have well over a dozen pairs now and i wash them on delicate cycle and hang dry. Only one pair( black) shows minimal piling on only the right leg seem. Still squat proof, sizing still seems pretty accurate and I don't think they have lost their stretch at all. I have found some Colors have a higher rise in the waist then others, but overall they all fit very similar. can wear both medium and large with no issues

Same fabric as Lulu for $30! The fabric on these leggings feels identical to my Lulus. Stitching on both sides of the leg but not in the front (no camel toe!). I bought a size S. I usually wear 4-6. 5'6" 140ish lbs. Good compression and a smooth physique in them. They are high waisted and the stitching on the back is flattering. So far through my workout and sitting down doing legos with my son they didn't roll down. Average ankle length. I usually like to pull my leggings over my heels depending on my workout (barre/pilates). These definitely can't stretch that far but I will order more. Squat-tested and can't see through them either! Solid purchase for only $30. Going to buy a couple more!

Super Cute, but My Thick Girls Size up!!! There’s some stretch to the pants. Size up if you’re thicker, it stretches where it shows a bit white n skin (as u can see in the pic I attached) and the stretch isn’t all that much when you are bottom heavy. Also may I add, if u have a lower belly the top part of the pants will roll down. It does compress n hides any signs of cellulite. But it gets annoying when constantly having to adjust them. I believe if I had sized up I would not have this issue. Unfortunately these were a tad too tight so remember my thick beauties to size up. If it helps I weigh 203 lbs and I’m 5’2.

Exceeded my expectations Really great leggings. I got the polka dot ones and another pair in black. Both fit perfect, nice compression without losing flexibility. Squat proof! No camel toe. I did also order the light blue ones but you could see more than I’d like to show. Not that they were not squat proof or gave me a cameltoe, but you could definitely see the outline even with seamless panties. Other girls may not have this problem and they were still great quality like the ones I am going to keep, just not for me. Highly recommend even though I’ll be sending the blue ones back. I am around 135-140 lbs, 5’7, usually size 4-6 in pants. I ordered size small.
